TXT_KEY_ACTION_AUTOMATE_BUILD_HELP: Automate all [COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]Worker[ENDCOLOR] actions. The unit will continue working until you cancel its automation.
TXT_KEY_ACTION_AUTOMATE_EXPLORE_DISABLED: No reachable areas to explore at the moment.
TXT_KEY_ACTION_AUTOMATE_EXPLORE_HELP: This order tells the unit to explore the uncharted regions on the map. The unit will continue to move until it has run out of places to explore.
TXT_KEY_ADVISOR_ESPIONAGE_CANT_STEAL_ANY: What if there is no more technology to steal?

After your spy has established surveillance in a target civilization, they will automatically start "Gathering Intelligence". If they discover that there are no technologies that you need, then you will receive a notification to let you know. You can then choose to move your spy to a better target, or instead leave your spy in the target civilization to watch for Intrigue. Intrigue is intercepted information that you can deliver to other civilizations. For example, you may have a spy in Arabia, and he may discover that they are planning to invade Egypt. You can then inform Egypt of this plot, and receive a diplomatic relationship boost as a result.
TXT_KEY_ADVISOR_ESPIONAGE_TECH_STOLEN_QU: How do I use spies to steal technology?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_CONTACTING_ADV_QUEST: How can I get my advisors on the horn?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_CONTACTING_HEADING2_BOD: During play, your Advisors will appear in "pop-ups" when they have something they think you should know. They'll often provide links to other information that relates to the current topic. You can follow these links or click "Thank You" at any time to dismiss the Advisor.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]You can also press the "Advisors" button in the upper right-hand corner of the screen to reach the "Advisor Counsel" screen. There you can see all four of your Advisors; if they have anything useful to say on the current situation it will appear there.
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_ECONOMIC_ADV_QUEST: Why is the economic advisor so great?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_ECONOMIC_HEADING3_BODY: The Economic Advisor provides advice on building and improving your cities and territory.
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_FOREIGN_ADV_QUEST: The foreign advisor is one pretty girl, but how can she help me?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_FOREIGN_HEADING3_BODY: The Foreign Advisor advises you on exploration and your relations with city-states, and other civilizations.
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_HEADING1_BODY: As leader of a mighty civilization, you have an able coterie of Advisors who will assist you with guidance and advice. They'll point out things that they believe are important, or that you might have forgotten about. You can turn them off if you like, but you may want to try playing with them for a while first. You have four different Advisors. Each provides advice on a specific area of expertise:
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_MILITARY_ADV_QUEST: Why is the military advisor so totally awesome?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_MILITARY_HEADING3_BODY: The Military Advisor provides advice on combat and all things related to war.
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_SCIENCE_ADV_QUEST: This science guy looks nerdy. Does Poindexer do anything cool?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_SCIENCE_HEADING3_BODY: The Science Advisor gives you advice on science and technology, as well as information on game rules.
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_TURNINGOFF_ADV_QUEST: How can I get the advisors to shut up?
TXT_KEY_ADVISORS_TURNINGOFF_HEADING2_BOD: You can determine how much assistance you get from the Advisors on the "Options" screen. You can set the advice level to New to Civ, New to Civ 5, Experienced Player, or No Advice. If turned off, they won't ever appear in pop-ups, but you can still go to the "Advisor Counsel" screen to see what they're thinking.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_CAPITAL_NEED_SETT: {1_CityName:textkey} is primed and ready to build a settler to expand our empire.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_CAPITAL_UNDER_THR: Keep watch around {1_CityName:textkey}, our capital city. Enemies have been seen in the area and we should be prepared for an attack.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_ENOUGH_NAVAL_TILE: {1_CityName:textkey} has all of its naval resources improved.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_ENOUGH_TILE_IMPRO: I don't think {1_CityName:textkey} needs to build any more Workers right now.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_HAVE_TRAINING_FAC: {1_CityName:textkey} has buildings that enhance our military production. We should build new units for our army in {1_CityName:textkey} to be most effective.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_LANDLOCKED: {1_CityName:textkey} is landlocked, which means it doesn't have access to the water. So you have to worry more about bears than sharks.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_NEED_IMPROVEMENT_: {1_CityName:textkey} needs [ICON_FOOD]food to grow to be a larger city. Use your Workers to build farms nearby.
{1_CityName:textkey} needs [ICON_PRODUCTION]production to build things faster. Use your Workers to build mines nearby.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_NEED_NAVAL_GROWTH: {1_CityName:textkey} has a considerable number of water tiles. We should focus on constructing buildings like the lighthouse that improve yield from those tiles.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_NEED_NAVAL_TILE_I: {1_CityName:textkey} has unimproved resources in the water; we should get a work boat to improve the resource!
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_NEED_TILE_IMPROVE: {1_CityName:textkey} needs Workers to improve nearby tiles. We should train a Worker in {1_CityName:textkey} and have them improve the nearby tiles.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_SMALL_CITY: Your city of {1_CityName:textkey} is too small at this point to undertake large constructions like wonders. Focus on growing {1_CityName:textkey} to a larger size before taking on big projects.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_UNDER_BLOCKADE: Enemies have been trying to blockade {1_CityName:textkey}! Use ranged attacks by the city and nearby boats to drive off the enemy ships that may blockade you.
TXT_KEY_AICITYSTRATEGY_WANT_TILE_IMPROVE: {1_CityName:textkey} would benefit greatly from having the surrounding tiles improved. Consider getting a Worker to help it out.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRBASES_HEADING2_BODY: Air units do not move around the map like ground and naval units. They must be "based" on a player-owned friendly city. Fighters and some bombers can be based upon aircraft carriers as well. Missile units may be based on cities, nuclear submarines, and missile cruisers. Air units cannot embark. If on extended sea voyages, they must be carried by the appropriate vessel - carrier, cruiser, or nuclear sub.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Anti-air units and helicopters don't need to be based. They move around the map like other units, and can embark.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRRANGE_ADV_QUEST: How does range for air units work?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRRANGE_HEADING2_BODY: In place of a movement stat, air units have "range." This is the distance from a base that they can perform "missions." It's also the distance that they can "rebase" - move from one base to another. For example, a fighter unit has a range of 8. It can perform its missions against any tile within 8 spaces of its current base, and it can move to another player owned base (city or carrier) that is within 8 spaces as well.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]An air unit that rebases cannot perform another mission in the same turn.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSTRIKE_HEADING3_BODY: The air unit attacks a ground target within its air range.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSTRIKES_HEADING2_BOD: When a unit is ordered to make an "air strike" against an enemy city or unit, if it is not intercepted, it performs a ranged attack against the target. Unlike most ranged attacks, however, the attacking unit can take damage from the attack. (In another words, if you bomb a tank, it might hurt you.) If the air striking unit survives, it returns to its base.[NEWLINE]Missiles and bombers have the most powerful air strikes.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSTRIKES_RULES_ADV_QU: What are the rules for air strikes?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSWEEP_HEADING3_BODY: The air unit "sweeps" a target tile, disabling "intercepting" units.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSWEEPS_HEADING3_BODY: When attacking a target that is heavily protected by fighters and AA units, a fighter can perform the "air sweep" mission against that target to "use up" the enemy's interception capability. If the fighter is intercepted by an enemy fighter, the two units dogfight, and one or the other might be damaged or destroyed. If the fighter is intercepted by a ground unit, it will take damage from the ground unit (but less so than a unit on an air strike mission would).
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRSWEEPS_RULES_ADV_QUE: What are the rules for air sweeps?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_AIRUNITS_HEADING2_BODY: There are four main types of air units in Civilization V: helicopter gunships, missiles, fighters, and bombers. Helicopter gunships are the closest to standard ground combat units and will be discussed separately.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Of the other air units, missiles are essentially "one-shot" weapons: you fire 'em, they hit their target and they're gone. Fighters are primarily used to defend against other air power and to clear the target of interceptors to allow bombers to hit their targets. Bombers do damage to targets on the ground, if not intercepted. Anti-air units defend against fighters and bombers.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ALLMISSILES_HEADING4_BO: Missiles can be based on player-owned cities, on missile cruisers and on nuclear submarines.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ANTITANKBONUS_ADV_QUEST: Just how good are gunship helicopters against tanks?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ANTITANKBONUS_HEADING3_: Gunships can do serious damage to tank units (which is why most modern armor is accompanied by hefty anti-air assets on the modern battlefield).
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ATOMIC_ADV_QUEST: Where can atomic bombers be based?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ATOMIC_HEADING4_BODY: Atomic bombers can be based on cities and on aircraft carriers.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ATOMICBOMBS_HEADING2_BO: The first nuclear weapon available in the game, the atomic bomb is a very powerful unit that can quickly alter the world's balance of power. Atomic bombs are similar to missiles in that they may only attack once. Unlike missiles, they may be stationed on aircraft carriers. The damage radius for the atomic bomb is 2 tiles in every direction. Units caught in the blast will be damaged, and cities will lose population if hit.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]An "A-bomb" cannot totally destroy a city. No matter how many times a city is hit by atomic bombs, it will not be destroyed.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_ATOMICBOMBS_RULES_ADV_Q: What are the rules for atomic bombs?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_BASELOCATIONS_ADV_QUEST: How do I know where a certain air unit can be based?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_BASELOCATIONS_HEADING3_: Different types of crafts may be based in different locations.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_BOMBERS_HEADING4_BODY: Bombers can be based on cities and aircraft carriers.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_CARRIER_ADV_QUEST: What air units can a carrier base?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_CARRIER_HEADING4_BODY: A carrier can carry up to three air units (fighters, bombers and atomic bombers).
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_CRUISER_ADV_QUEST: What air units can a missile cruiser base?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_CRUISER_HEADING4_BODY: A missile cruiser can carry up to three missiles of any type.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_FIGHTERS_ADV_QUEST: Where can fighters and jet fighters be based?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_FIGHTERS_HEADING4_BODY: Fighters and jet fighters can be based on cities and aircraft carriers.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_HEADING1_BODY: By the Second World War, air power has come to dominate warfare around the world. Air power acts as a "force multiplier" in combat, and the nation that can establish air supremacy over the battlefield has a huge advantage over the enemy.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Perhaps most importantly, strategic bombing has become a central force in modern warfare, and with the advent of nuclear ballistic missiles, it has the ability to literally wipe an entire civilization off the face of the planet.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Air power is critical to victory in Civilization V.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_HELIGUNSHIPS_HEADING2_B: As stated above, helicopter gunships are quite similar to standard land units. They are extremely effective at killing tanks, but remain vulnerable to anti-aircraft fire.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_INTERCEPTION_ADV_QUEST: How does interception work with air units?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_INTERCEPTION_HEADING2_B: Fighters and jet fighters can be set to "intercept" enemy air attacks. (AA gun, mobile SAM, and certain naval units automatically intercept; they don't need to be given a special mission.) If an air unit tries an air strike against a target within the range of an intercepting unit, the interceptor will fire on the attacker and do damage to it. Unless the attacker is killed by the interceptor, the air strike proceeds.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Only one unit can intercept an air strike, and once it does so, it cannot intercept any more that turn (though certain promotions can increase this). So if you're expecting multiple air attacks on a target, you might want to pile multiple fighters and AA units on and around that target.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_INTERCEPTION_HEADING3_B: The air unit prepares to defend against enemy air attacks. (Note that ground-based anti-air units automatically intercept when attacked by air; they don't need special orders.)
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_INTERCEPTION_RULES_ADV_: What are the rules for interception?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_MISSILES_HEADING2_BODY: Missiles are one-shot weapons. They perform a single air strike mission against a target, and then, win or lose, they are destroyed. Unlike normal aircraft, missiles cannot be intercepted.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_MISSILES_RULES_ADV_QUES: What are the rules for missiles?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_MISSIONS_HEADING2_BODY: During an air unit's turn, it can perform one of a number of "missions." These include making air strikes (ranged attacks against ground targets), rebasing, interception (defending against enemy air attack) and "sweeping" (disabling enemy interception). Some air units can perform only some of these missions.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_MOVEMENT_ADV_QUEST: What are the rules on gunship helicopter movement?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_MOVEMENT_HEADING3_BODY: Gunships can move over all terrain types including mountains and ice, at a cost of 1 MP each. They can also move over coastal waters and deep water if they embark.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NAVALUNIT_ADV_QUEST: Which naval units can be bases for air units?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NAVALUNIT_HEADING3_BODY: Some naval units can be used as bases for air units and these naval units may hold more than one air unit.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NUCMISSILES_HEADING2_BO: While the atomic bomb does a fair amount of damage, nuclear missiles are truly devastating. All units caught in the blast radius are destroyed, and the damage done to cities is even greater. Unlike atomic bombs, nuclear missiles are even capable of wiping cities off the map completely (note that a player's original capital may not be destroyed in this manner).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]As their name implies, nuclear missiles are missile units, which means they may be stationed on missile cruisers or nuclear submarines, but not aircraft carriers.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NUCMISSILES_RULES_ADV_Q: What are the rules for nuclear missiles?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NUCSUBMARINE_ADV_QUEST: What air units can a nuclear submarine base?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_NUCSUBMARINE_HEADING4_B: A nuclear submarine can carry up to two missiles of any type.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_REBASE_ADV_QUEST: How can an air unit change bases?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_REBASE_HEADING3_BODY: The air unit moves to a new base within its range.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_RECON_ADV_QUEST: What does the recon ability for air units do?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_RECON_HEADING2_BODY: Fighters, jet fighters and stealth bombers have a special "recon" ability. At the start of their turn, everything within 6 tiles of their base is visible. This isn't a mission and it doesn't use up their turn: it happens automatically.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_STACKING_ADV_QUEST: What are the stacking rules with air units?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_STACKING_HEADING3_BODY: You can have any number of missiles, fighters and bombers in a single tile. There is no "stacking" limitation for these units. They can be stacked with both combat and non-combat units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Helicopters and anti-air units must follow the standard stacking rules.
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_STEALTH_ADV_QUEST: Where can stealth bombers be based?
TXT_KEY_AIRPOWER_STEALTH_HEADING4_BODY: Stealth bombers can be based on cities only.
TXT_KEY_B685D5DE7CCA4E7581B42F60754E6330: Isabella's Spain and Pachacuti's Incan Empire
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_ADV_QUEST: What are barbarians and ancient ruins?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_ANCIENTRUINS_ADV_QUEST: What are ancient ruins and what do they do?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_ANCIENTRUINS_HEADING2_: Ancient Ruins are the remnants of even earlier civilizations which rose and fell long before you came on the scene. Ruins provide a random benefit to the civilization of the unit that first enters their tile. The ruin is destroyed when it is entered.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Ruins are cool. Find as many as you can before other civs get to them!
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_BARBARIANS_HEADING2_BO: Barbarians are roving bands of villains who hate civilization and everything that goes with it. They attack your units and cities and pillage your improvements. They're just not very nice at all.[NEWLINE]As your civilization grows the barbarians become much less menacing, but early in the game they can be a huge problem.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_CAPTURED_ADV_QUEST: What happens if a barbarian captures a civilian unit?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_CAPTURED_HEADING3_BODY: If a barbarian unit comes upon a non-combat unit - settler or worker - the barbarians capture that unit. They will take it off to their nearest encampment, and the unit may be recovered by any player in the game. Should one of your civilians be captured in this manner, be sure to pursue and retrieve them before somebody else does! Great people are the exception. Barbarians will destroy a great person if they are found undefended.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_CULTURE_ADV_QUEST: How can an ancient ruin provide you with culture?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_CULTURE_HEADING4_BODY: The ruin provides culture to your civilization.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_DESTROYING_ADV_QUEST: What do you get if you destroy a barbarian encampment?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_DESTROYING_HEADING4_BO: A civilization will earn a gold reward for dispersing a barbarian encampment - in addition to the benefit of stopping it from spawning more barbarian units, which of course is the primary reward.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_ENCAMPMENTS_HEADING3_B: Barbarians come from "encampments", which may appear randomly in any tile that cannot be seen by a unit. Every few turns the encampment will create another barbarian unit which will make a beeline for the nearest civilization and start causing trouble. The only way to stop this is to find the encampment and destroy it. Encampments are usually guarded by at least one unit, so they're not pushovers.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_FREETECH_ADV_QUEST: How can an ancient ruin help you with technology?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_FREETECH_HEADING4_BODY: The ruin provides your civilization with a free technology.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_HEADING1_BODY: During the early portion of the game - say, the first 25 - 50 turns - much of your energy should be involved in exploring the world. During your exploration you will be encountering ancient ruins and barbarians. Ancient ruins are good, barbarians are not.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_MAP_ADV_QUEST: How can an ancient ruin help you explore the map?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_MAP_HEADING4_BODY: The ruin provides a map of the surrounding area (lifting the fog of war from a number of tiles).
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_NAVALUNITS_ADV_QUEST: What are these barbarians doing with boats!?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_NAVALUNITS_HEADING3_BO: Barbarian encampments on the coastline can create naval units (again, equal to those that can be created by the civilization with the most advanced tech). These units will menace your coastline, destroy naval improvements and attack hapless land units which stray too near the coastline. It's important to maintain a navy to keep them off your back, but the best way to stop these attacks is to destroy the coastal encampments nearby.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Warning: If a barbarian ship comes across an embarked unit, it will destroy it!
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_NEWENCAMPMENTS_ADV_QUE: How do new barbarian encampments show up?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_NEWENCAMPMENTS_HEADING: Barbarian encampments may spring up in any neutral space which cannot be seen by a civilization's city or unit. If you want to keep barbarian encampments from popping up around your civilization, place units on hills to keep as much terrain in sight as possible.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_POINTLIMITS_ADV_QUEST: How are unit experience points gained when battling barbarians?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_POINTLIMITS_HEADING3_B: When they fight barbarian units, your less well-trained units will gain experience points. However, any unit that has already acquired 30 XPs (or has exchanged that many for promotions) no longer gains XPs from fighting barbarians.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_RUINBENEFITS_ADV_QUEST: What benefits can you get from an ancient ruin?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_RUINBENEFITS_HEADING3_: Ancient ruins can provide a number of different benefits.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_SURVIVORS_ADV_QUEST: How can your cities gain population from an ancient ruin?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_SURVIVORS_HEADING4_BOD: The ruin contains survivors from the earlier civilization. They move to one of your cities, increasing its population by one.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_THEEND_ADV_QUEST: When will the barbarians be gone?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_THEEND_HEADING2_BODY: Barbarians can remain in the game right up until the end. However, as more land is acquired there will be less available for the barbarian encampments to spawn in. If the entire world is civilized, the barbarians will be gone.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_TREASURE_ADV_QUEST: How can an ancient ruin provide you with gold?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_TREASURE_HEADING4_BODY: The ruin provides gold to your civilization!
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_UNITS_ADV_QUEST: What kind of units can barbarians use?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_UNITS_HEADING2_BODY: Barbarian encampments can create almost any kind of unit in the game - from warriors and spearmen to cannon and tanks. (They can build units equal to those that the most advanced civilization can create.)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Once created the barbarian units will either hang around their encampment or head off toward the nearest civilization or city-state and try to cause trouble. They'll attack units, destroy improvements and menace cities. If enough are involved, they can take down a poorly-defended city, which is then thoroughly pillaged. A pillaged city may lose gold, buildings and population.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]This is why it's important to periodically sweep the countryside around your civilization, destroying encampments before they become a threat.
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_WEAPONS_ADV_QUEST: How can an ancient ruin upgrade one of your units?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_WEAPONS_HEADING4_BODY: The unit which enters the tile is upgraded to a more advanced unit (a warrior might become a swordsman, for example).
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_WORKERS_ADV_QUEST: Can you get settler and worker from ancient ruins?
TXT_KEY_BARBARIAN_WORKERS_HEADING4_BODY: On easier difficulty levels, you can also receive free Settlers and Workers from ruins.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_ASCETISM: Shrines provide +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness in cities with 3 followers
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_CATHEDRALS: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase Cathedrals (+1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith, +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture, +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness, 1 Great Work of Art slot)
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_CEREMONIAL_BURIAL: +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Global Happiness for every two cities following this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_CHARITABLE_MISSIONS: Influence boosts from [ICON_GOLD] Gold gifts to City-States are increased by 30%.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_CHORAL_MUSIC: Temples provide +2 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture in cities with 5 followers
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_CHURCH_PROPERTY: +2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for each city following this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_DANCE_AURORA: +1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith from Tundra tiles without Forest
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_DEFENDER_FAITH: +20% [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength near friendly cities that follow this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_DIVINE_INSPIRATION: Each World Wonder provides +2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith in city
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_EARTH_MOTHER: +1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each Copper, Iron, and Salt resource
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_EVANGELISM: Missionaries' Spread Religion action erodes existing pressure from other religions.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_FAITH_HEALERS: +30 HP healed per turn if adjacent to a friendly city
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_FEED_WORLD: Shrines and Temples provide +1 [ICON_FOOD] Food each in city
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_FORMAL_LITURGY: +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and +1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each Gold and Silver
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GOD_CRAFTSMEN: +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production in cities with population of 3+
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GOD_FESTIVALS: +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and +1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each Wine and Incense
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GOD_WAR: Gain [ICON_PEACE] Faith if you win a battle within 4 tiles of your city
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GODDESS_LOVE: +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness from cities with population of 6+
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GODDESS_STRATEGY: +30% increase in city [ICON_RANGE_STRENGTH] Ranged Combat Strength
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_GURUSHIP: +2 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production if city has a Specialist
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_HEATHEN_CONVERSION: Missionaries convert adjacent Barbarian units to this civilization.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_HOLY_ORDER: Missionaries and Inquisitors cost 30% less [ICON_PEACE] Faith
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_HOLY_WARRIORS: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase pre-Industrial land units
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_INITIATION_RITES: +100 [ICON_GOLD] Gold when each city first converts to this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_INTERFAITH_DIALOGUE: Gain [ICON_RESEARCH] Science when a Missionary or Prophet spreads this religion to cities of other religions
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_ITINERANT_PREACHERS: Religion spreads to cities 30% further away
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_JESUIT_EDUCATION: May build Universities, Public Schools, and Research Labs with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_JUST_WAR: +20% [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength near enemy cities that follow this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_LITURGICAL_DRAMA: Ampitheaters provide +1 [ICON_PEACE] Faith in cities with 3 followers
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_MESSENGER_GODS: +2 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science in cities with a [ICON_CONNECTED] City Connection
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_MESSIAH: Prophets 25% stronger and earned with 25% less [ICON_PEACE] Faith
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_MONASTERIES: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase Monasteries (+2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith, +2 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture, more with [ICON_RES_WINE] Wine or [ICON_RES_INCENSE] Incense)
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_MOSQUES: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase Mosques (+3 [ICON_PEACE] Faith, +2 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture, +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness)
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_PAGODAS: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase Pagodas (+2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith, +2 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture, +2 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness)
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_PAPAL_PRIMACY: +15 to [ICON_INFLUENCE] Influence resting point with City-States following this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_PEACE_LOVING: +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Global Happiness for every 8 followers of this religion in non-enemy foreign cities
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_PILGRIMAGE: +2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each foreign city following this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_RELIGIOUS_CENTER: Temples provide +2 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness in cities with 5 followers
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_RELIGIOUS_FERVOR: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase Industrial Era (and later) land units.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_RELIGIOUS_TEXTS: Religion spreads 25% faster (50% with Printing Press)
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_RELIGIOUS_UNITY: Religion spreads to friendly city-states at double rate
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_RELIQUARY: Gain 50 [ICON_PEACE] Faith each time a Great Person is expended
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_SACRED_SITES: All buildings purchased with [ICON_PEACE] Faith provide 2 [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism each.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_SUN_GOD: +1 [ICON_FOOD] Food for each Banana, Citrus, and Wheat resource
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_SWORD_PLOWSHARES: 15% faster [ICON_FOOD] Growth Rate for city if not at war
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_TEARS_OF_GODS: +2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each Gems and Pearls resource
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_TITHE: +1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for every 4 followers of this religion
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_TO_GLORY_OF_GOD: Use [ICON_PEACE] Faith to purchase any type of [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person starting in Industrial Era.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_UNDERGROUND_SECT: Your spies exert religious pressure on the cities they occupy.
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_UNITY_OF_PROPHETS: Inquisitors and Prophets reduce this religion's presence by half (instead of eliminating it).
TXT_KEY_BELIEF_WORLD_CHURCH: +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture for every 5 followers of this religion in other civilizations
TXT_KEY_BROADWAY_THEMING_BONUS_HELP: To maximize your bonus, make sure the Great Work Slots are all filled with Music from the same Era and Civilization.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_ARCHAEOLOGY_DIG_HELP: Converts the Antiquity Site into an Archaeological Dig, which can yield a Great Work Artifact or be transformed into a Landmark improvement.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_ARCHAEOLOGY_DIG_REC: It can yield a Great Work Artifact or be transformed into a Landmark improvement.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_BRAZILWOOD_CAMP_HELP: Constructs a Brazilwood Camp, which increases the amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold provided by this tile, while keeping the Jungle intact. Also provides [ICON_CULTURE] Culture once Acoustics has been researched.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_BRAZILWOOD_CAMP_REC: It will increase the amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold provided by this tile, while keeping the Jungle intact. Also provides [ICON_CULTURE] Culture once Acoustics has been researched.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_CHATEAU_HELP: Constructs a Chateau which increases the Culture and Gold yield of the tile. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_CHATEAU_REC: A Chateau must be built adjacent to a Luxury resource. It may not be adjacent to another Chateau.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]It will increase the Culture and Gold yield of the tile. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_CITADEL_HELP: +100% [ICON_STRENGTH] Defensive Strength for any Unit stationed in this Tile. Any enemy unit which ends its turn next to the Citadel takes 3 damage. Constructing the Citadel will consume this unit.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_CONSUMED_HELP: Unit will be consumed.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Great Tile Improvements will connect Strategic Resources on this tile to the trade network.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_FEITORIA_HELP: Constructs a Feitoria in this City-State's lands. It will provide to you one copy of every Luxury Resource type connected by this City-State, regardless of your relationship with the City-State. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_FEITORIA_REC: It will provide to you one copy of every Luxury Resource type connected by this City-State, regardless of your relationship with the City-State. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_FOOD_REC: It will increase the amount of [ICON_FOOD] Food provided by this tile, speeding up the birth of new [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_FORT_HELP: +50% [ICON_STRENGTH] Defensive Strength for any Unit stationed in this Tile.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_FORT_REC: It will improve the [ICON_STRENGTH] Defense of any military unit stationed in this tile.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_HOLY_SITE_HELP: Unit will be consumed.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Great Tile Improvements will connect Strategic Resources on this tile to the trade network.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_KASBAH_HELP: Constructs a Kasbah, which increases the amount of [ICON_FOOD] Food, [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, and [ICON_GOLD] Gold provided by this tile. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_KASBAH_REC: It will increase the amount of [ICON_FOOD] Food, [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, and [ICON_GOLD] Gold provided by this tile. Also provides a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_LANDMARK_HELP: Unit will be consumed.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]+6 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Great Tile Improvements will connect Strategic Resources on this tile to the trade network.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_LUMBERMILL_REC: It will increase the amount of [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production provided by this tile, while keeping the Forest intact.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_MOAI_HELP: Adds a Moai (+1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture) to the tile. Create a line of Moai along the coast for additional culture. Also provides +1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold after Flight is researched.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_MOAI_REC: It will permit the tile to generate [ICON_CULTURE] Culture (and [ICON_GOLD] after Flight is researched).
TXT_KEY_BUILD_RAILROAD_HELP: [COLOR_NEGATIVE_TEXT]Costs[ENDCOLOR] 2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold per turn to maintain.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]An unbroken chain of Railroads allows Units to move extremely quickly through Tiles.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Connecting Cities to your capital by Railroad will give them a 25% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production bonus.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_RAILROAD_REC: It will allow units to move more quickly across the terrain, and connecting other cities to your [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital will grant them additional [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production!
TXT_KEY_BUILD_REMOVE_FEATURE_HELP: Allows for another Improvement to be constructed on this Tile.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_ROAD_HELP: [COLOR_NEGATIVE_TEXT]Costs[ENDCOLOR] 1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold per turn to maintain.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]An unbroken chain of Roads allows Units to move more quickly across the map.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Connecting Cities to your capital will provide a large amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold per turn.
TXT_KEY_BUILD_ROAD_REC: It will allow units to move more quickly across the terrain, and connecting other cities to your [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital will produce additional [ICON_GOLD] Gold!
TXT_KEY_BUILD_SCRUB_FALLOUT_HELP: Removes the nuclear waste present in this Tile.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_AIRPORT_HELP: Allows airlifts to or from this city. [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Increases air unit capacity of the city from 6 to 10.[ENDCOLOR] 50% of the [ICON_CULTURE] Culture from World Wonders, Natural Wonders, and Improvements (Landmarks, Moai, Chateau) is added to the [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism output of the city. [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism output from Great Works +50%.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_AIRPORT_STRATEGY: The versatile Airport is an excellent choice for both civilizations that are trying to improve their [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism output or and those that need to use airlift capabilites to shuttle units to distant cities.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_AMPHITHEATER_STRATEGY: The Amphitheater increases the [ICON_CULTURE] Culture of a city, speeding the growth of the city's territory and the civilization's acquisition of Social Policies. The city must contain a Monument (or a Stele) before the Amphitheater can be constructed. Contains 1 slot for a Great Work of Writing.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_AQUEDUCT_HELP: 40% of [ICON_FOOD] Food is carried over after a new [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizen is born.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_AQUEDUCT_STRATEGY: The Aqueduct decreases the amount of [ICON_FOOD] Food a city needs to increase in size by 40%. Build Aqueducts in cities that you want to grow large over time.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARMORY_HELP: +15 XP for all Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a Barracks or Krepost.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARMORY_STRATEGY: The Armory provides experience for military units constructed in the city. The city must contain a Barracks before an Armory can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARSENAL_HELP: City must have a Castle or Mughal Fort.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARSENAL_STRATEGY: The Arsenal is a mid-game military building that increases a city's Defense Strength, making the city more difficult to capture. The city must possess a Castle before it can construct an Arsenal.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARTISTS_GUILD_HELP: Provides 2 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points (GPP) towards a Great Artist. Add up to two specialists to this building to gain [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and increase the rate of Great Artist acquisition (3 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] GPP per specialist).
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ARTISTS_GUILD_STRATEGY: The Artists' Guild is the only building that contains artist specialist slots, which are critical for generating Great Artists. If your goal is a cultural victory, this building is a must-have. The Artists' Guild provides 2 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points, and each assigned specialist provides 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points and 3 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BANK_STRATEGY: The Bank is a Renaissance-era building which increases the city's output of [ICON_GOLD] Gold. A city must possess a Market or Bazaar before a Bank may be constructed. Trade routes other players make to a city with a Bank will generate an extra 1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the city owner and the trade route owner gains an additional 1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the trade route.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BARRACKS_STRATEGY: Barracks provide experience for military units constructed in the city. This experience can be used to give the units Promotions, making them more effective in battle.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BAZAAR_HELP: Provides 1 extra copy of each improved luxury resource near this City. Each source of [ICON_RES_OIL] Oil and each Oasis provides +2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BAZAAR_STRATEGY: The Bazaar is the Arabian unique building, replacing the Market. The Bazaar provides a [ICON_GOLD] Gold bonus to the city. The civilization gains one additional copy of each luxury resource provided by a city possessing a Bazaar. The Bazaar provides +2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold on [ICON_RES_OIL] Oil and Oases.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BOMB_SHELTER_HELP: Reduces population loss from nuclear attack by 75%.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BOMB_SHELTER_STRATEGY: Bomb Shelters reduce population loss from a nuclear attack on this city by 75%. Duck and Cover!
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BOROBUDUR_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"When you realize how perfect everything is, you will tilt your head back and laugh at the sky."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Gautama Buddha[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BROADCAST_TOWER_HELP: +33% [ICON_CULTURE] Culture in this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a Museum.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BROADCAST_TOWER_STRATEG: A Broadcast Tower is a late-game building which increases [ICON_CULTURE] Culture output of the city by 33%. The city must possess a Museum before it can construct a Broadcast Tower.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BROADWAY_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"Broadway is a main artery of New York cultural life - the hardened artery."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Walter Winchell[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BURIAL_TOMB_HELP: Requires no [ICON_GOLD] Gold maintenance.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Should this city be captured, the amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold plundered by the enemy is doubled.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BURIAL_TOMB_PEDIA: The Egyptians raised ancestor-worship to heights unmatched before or since, and much of their civilization's wealth and creative energy was expended in the construction of Burial Tombs for deceased relatives. Many such tombs may be found in the Valley of the Kings, across the Nile River from Thebes. These tombs were often stocked with a portion of the wealth of the deceased, making them rich targets for tomb robbers throughout history.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_BURIAL_TOMB_STRATEGY: The Burial Tomb is a Classical-era building which increases the city's output of [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness, and costs no maintenance for the city. However, if the city is captured, the capturing civilization will gain twice as much gold as would be the case in a city without a Burial Tomb. It replaces the Temple.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CANDI_HELP: Unique Indonesian Garden replacement. +25% [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People generation in this City, and +2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each World Religion that has at least 1 follower in the city.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Unlike the Garden the Candi does not have to be built next to a River or Lake.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CANDI_STRATEGY: In addition to the regular abilities of the Garden, the Candi also provides +2 [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each World Religion that has at least 1 follower in the city. Only Indonesia may build it, and the city does not need to be located next to a River or Lake.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CARAVANSARY_HELP: Land trade routes originating from this city have a 50% increase in range and produce an additional 2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold when connecting to another civilization.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CARAVANSARY_STRATEGY: The Caravansary is a Classical-era building. It improves the range and [ICON_GOLD] Gold yield of land trade routes.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CASTLE_STRATEGY: The Castle is a Medieval-era building which increases the city's Defensive Strength. The city must possess Walls before the Castle can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CATHEDRAL_STRATEGY: Can only be built in cities following a religion with the Cathedrals belief. Construct this building by purchasing it with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CEILIDH_HALL_STRATEGY: The Ceilidh Hall is a Renaissance-era building unique to the Celts, replacing the Opera House. It increases the city's [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CIRCUS_HELP: City must have an improved source of [ICON_RES_HORSE] Horses or [ICON_RES_IVORY] Ivory nearby.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CIRCUS_MAXIMUS_HELP: Must have built a Colosseum in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CIRCUS_MAXIMUS_PEDIA: A massive stadium located in the heart of Rome, the Circus Maximus was the first, and largest, chariot racing venue in ancient Rome. At the time of its usage, it could hold over a quarter of a million people, over 25% of the city's population. Measuring over 2000 ft in length, twelve full-sized chariots could easily race side by side. Originally used by the Etruscan kings to host their entertainments and festivals, the first Roman Games were held at the racetrack by the fifth Roman King, Tarquinius Priscus. The site was later expanded by Julius Caesar in 50 BC to its current size.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]The last recorded race was held in 549 AD by the Ostrogoth king Totila, and was later abandoned. Now the Circus is a public park, the track covered over in grass. Some of the original structures still remain, namely a few starting gates and medians, but now the Circus is mostly used to host live concerts and meetings on the open green.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CIRCUS_MAXIMUS_STRATEGY: This National Wonder increases [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness in the empire by 5. It also provides +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. A civilization must have a Colosseum in all cities before it can construct a Circus Maximus.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CIRCUS_STRATEGY: The Circus can only be constructed in a city with an improved source of [ICON_RES_HORSE] Horses or [ICON_RES_IVORY] Ivory nearby. The Circus increases a civilization's [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COFFEE_HOUSE_HELP: +25% [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People generation in this City.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COFFEE_HOUSE_STRATEGY: The Coffee House is a Renaissance-era building unique to Austria, replacing the Windmill. It increases the city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production and speed at which [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People are generated by 25%.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COLOSSEUM_STRATEGY: The Colosseum increases a civilization's [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness. It cannot provide more Happiness than there are [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in a city.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CONSTABLE_HELP: Reduces enemy spy stealing rate by 25%.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_CONSTABLE_STRATEGY: Constabularies slow down the rate at which enemy spies can steal technologies from your city.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COURTHOUSE_HELP: Eliminates extra [ICON_HAPPINESS_4] Unhappiness from an [ICON_OCCUPIED] Occupied City.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COURTHOUSE_PEDIA: A Courthouse is a building in which the local authorities administer the law. Oftentimes the building also houses the local civil government as well, but this is by no means always the case. Generally the courthouse occupies a conspicuous place of importance in the central part of the city - either because the citizens view it with pride, or because the government wants to remind the citizens that it's got its eye on them.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_COURTHOUSE_STRATEGY: The Courthouse reduces the unhappiness caused by captured cities. Construct one in each occupied city as quickly as possible, especially if you're intent upon conquering a bunch of them.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_DUCAL_STABLE_HELP: Unique Polish Stable replacement. +15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production and +15 XP for Mounted Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Each Pasture worked by this City produces +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production and +1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_DUCAL_STABLE_STRATEGY: In addition to the regular abilities of the Stable, the Ducal Stable increases [ICON_GOLD] Gold on Pasture tiles and +15 XP for Mounted Units. Only the Polish may build it.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_EAST_INDIA_STRATEGY: The East India Company increases the amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold a city generates. Trade routes other players make to a city with an East India Company will generate an extra 4 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the city owner and the trade route owner gains an additional 2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the trade route.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FACTORY_STRATEGY: The Factory is an Industrial-era building which greatly increases a city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production. The city must already possess a Workshop (or Longhouse). 1 [ICON_RES_COAL] Coal is consumed when a Factory is created.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FLOATING_GARDENS_HELP: +15% [ICON_FOOD] Food. Each worked Lake tile provides +2 [ICON_FOOD] Food.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built next to a River or Lake.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FLOATING_GARDENS_PEDIA: A floating garden is a small artificial island constructed on a freshwater lake in order to increase the land available for agriculture. The lake irrigates the island and provides it with fresh organic material, resulting in an incredibly fertile growing environment. The Aztecs were masters of this form of agriculture, and the "Floating Gardens of Xochimilco" remain famous today.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FOATING_GARDENS_STRATEG: The Floating Gardens is the Aztec unique building, replacing the Water Mill. It increases the city's [ICON_FOOD] Food output, and should be built if the city is near a lake or river.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FORGE_HELP: +15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Land Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Each source of [ICON_RES_IRON] Iron worked by this City produces +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a mined source of Iron nearby.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_FORGE_STRATEGY: The Forge can only be constructed in a city with an improved source of [ICON_RES_IRON] Iron nearby. It increases the speed at which the city produces land units.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GARDEN_HELP: +25% [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People generation in this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built next to a River or Lake.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GARDEN_STRATEGY: The Garden increases the speed at which [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People are generated in the city by 25%. Gardens can be constructed only in cities next to a river or lake.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GLOBE_THEATER_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"Totus mundus agit histrionem"[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Inscription above main entrance to the Globe Theatre[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GRANARY_HELP: Each source of [ICON_RES_WHEAT] Wheat [ICON_RES_BANANA] Bananas and [ICON_RES_DEER] Deer worked by this City produce +1 [ICON_FOOD] Food.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GRANARY_STRATEGY: The Granary increases the [ICON_FOOD] Food intake of a city, speeding up the city's growth. It's useful when you want to create a large city, fast.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GRAND_TEMPLE_HELP: Doubles religious pressure emanating from this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Must be built in a Holy City. Must have built a Temple in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GRAND_TEMPLE_STRATEGY: An important building for a civilization trying to spread their religion world-wide from an empire with few, populous cities. Must be built in a Holy City. Doubles religious pressure emanating from this city. A civilization must have a Temple in all cities before it can construct the Grand Temple.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GRAND_TEMPLE_TEXT: A Grand Temple is a revered holy site held sacred among the followers of a given religion. These temples often feature grand architecture and ornate inscriptions that stand for centuries as a testament to the faith of the religion's adherents. Although there is no clear consensus as to which of the many temples found throughout the world today is the oldest, many believe the Göbekli Tepe - found in modern Turkey and assembled over 12,000 years ago - to be the earliest man-made religious structure ever discovered.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_GREAT_FIREWALL_HELP: 99.9% reduction in effectiveness of enemy spies in the city in which it is built. All other cities in the civilization get a 25% reduction in enemy spy effectiveness. Negates the [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism bonus from other players' Internet technology.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HANSE_HELP: Unique German Bank replacement. +5% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production for each Trade Route your civilization has with a City-State. Trade routes other players make to a city with a Hanse will generate an extra 1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the city owner and the trade route owner gains an additional 1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold for the trade route.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HANSE_STRATEGY: In addition to the regular abilities of the Bank (additional [ICON_GOLD] Gold output, and additional [ICON_GOLD] Gold from Trade Routes), the Hanse provides [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production for each Trade Route within your civilization that connects to a City-State. The Trade Routes can come from any combination of cities, even cities without the Hanse (Example: If you have trade routes from Berlin to Geneva, Munich to Geneva, Munich to Berlin, and Berlin to Brussels, then all cities with the Hanse would get +15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production). Only Germany may build it.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HARBOR_HELP: Forms a [ICON_CONNECTED] Trade Route with the [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital over water (if they're not already [ICON_CONNECTED] connected by land). +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from Sea Resources worked by this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built on the coast.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HARBOR_PEDIA: A harbor is a sheltered body of water that provides protection from the elements for boats and ships at anchor within. Where there are some "natural" harbors, most have required some degree of dredging, the construction of breakwaters and windbreaks, and the like. In addition, harbors also possess docks and jetties for loading and unloading cargo as well as warehouses to store the goods passing through.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HARBOR_STRATEGY: The Harbor is a Medieval-era building. It increases the city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production of naval units and it allows "water routes" (see rules) between cities. The Harbor also increases the [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production output of an improved sea resource.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HERMITAGE_HELP: +50% [ICON_CULTURE] Culture in this City. [NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Must have built an Opera House in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HERMITAGE_PEDIA: The Hermitage is a museum in Saint Petersburg, Russia. Constructed in 1764 by Catherine the Great, the Hermitage's collection contains some three million pieces, including the largest collection of paintings in the world. The collection is housed in a number of historic buildings, including the extraordinary Winter Palace, the former official residence of the Tsars from 1732 to 1917.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HERMITAGE_STRATEGY: This National Wonder increases the cultural output of the city in which it is constructed by 50%. It cannot be constructed unless the civilization has an Opera House built in all of its cities.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HEROIC_EPIC_HELP: All newly-trained non-air Units in this City receive the [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Morale[ENDCOLOR] Promotion, improving [ICON_STRENGTH] combat strength by 15%.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Must have built a Barracks in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HEROIC_EPIC_PEDIA: From ancient times, a "heroic epic" is a poem that defines a people or an era, and is usually centered upon the heroic actions of one or more people. The "Odyssey" is such a work, as is the Mesopotamian "Epic of Gilgamesh" and the Anglo-Saxon "Beowulf."
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HEROIC_EPIC_STRATEGY: This National Wonder gives all units built in this city the "Morale" promotion, increasing their combat strength by +15%. It also provides +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. The Heroic Epic can be constructed when a civilization has a barracks in all of its cities.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HOSPITAL_PEDIA: A hospital is an institution for diagnosing and treating the sick or injured, and for housing them during their recovery. Many hospitals are also teaching institutions, where student doctors and nurses learn their trade. The earliest hospitals were temples to certain gods associated with health and healing. Religious hospitals were established in Sri Lanka by 430 BC, and King Asoka of India created a chain of hospitals across Hindustan around 230 BC.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HOSPITAL_STRATEGY: The Hospital is an Industrial-era building. It's useful when you want to create a large city, fast.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HOTEL_HELP: 50% of the [ICON_CULTURE] Culture from World Wonders, Natural Wonders, and Improvements (Landmarks, Moai, Chateau) is added to the [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism output of the city. [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism output from Great Works +50%.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HOTEL_STRATEGY: With its massive boost to [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism, the Hotel is an excellent choice for civilizations that are trying to achieve a Culture victory, or attempting to increase your ideological impact on other civilizations.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HYDRO_PLANT_HELP: +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production in every tile next to a River. Requires 1 [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built next to a River.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_HYDRO_PLANT_STRATEGY: The Hydro Plant is a Modern-era building which can only be constructed in a city which is adjacent to a river. The Hydro Plant increases the [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production output of every tile the city works which is also adjacent to the river. Constructing the Hydro Plant consumes 1 [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_IKANDA_HELP: Unique Zulu Barracks replacement. Grants unique promotions to pre-gunpowder melee units.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_IKANDA_STRATEGY: In addition to the regular abilities of the Barracks which it replaces, the Ikanda grants a unique set of promotions to pre-gunpowder melee units created within the city. These include faster movement, and greater combat strength. Only the Zulu may build it.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_INTELLIGENCE_AGENCY_HEL: Provides an additional spy and levels up all your existing spies. Also provides a 15% reduction in enemy spy effectiveness. Must have a Police Station in all cities.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_INTELLIGENCE_AGENCY_STR: An important defensive National Wonder for a technology-driven civilization. The National Intelligence Agency provides an additional spy, levels up all your existing spies, and provides a 15% reduction in enemy spy effectiveness. A civilization must have a Police Station in all cities before it can construct the National Intelligence Agency.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_INTELLIGENCE_AGENCY_TEX: A National Intelligence Agency provides government resources for the acquisition of information and intelligence data key to providing security and national defense. Everything from covert operations, including espionage and infiltration, to the assessment of national security risks and response recommendations are made by these agencies. The Central Intelligence Agency of the United States is perhaps the most famous (or notorious) of the world's intelligence agencies, as the program has continually influenced global affairs through various means for decades.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_INTERNATIONAL_SPACE_STA: +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from Scientists, and +1 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science from Engineers. Great Scientists provide 33% more [ICON_RESEARCH] Science when used to discover new technology. May only be built collaboratively through the World Congress.
[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"Building one space station for everyone was and is insane; we should have built a dozen."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Larry Niven[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_IRONWORKS_HELP: Must have built a Workshop in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_IRONWORKS_PEDIA: An ironworks is a facility where iron ore is purified and fashioned into iron or steel. The process begins with exposing the raw material to extremely high heat, and after possibly mixing in other materials, results in some form of purified iron or steel. Iron smelting dates back at least to 1200 BC, and perhaps earlier. Not surprisingly, ironworks are dangerous places, and they require tough, highly-skilled and courageous workers.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_IRONWORKS_STRATEGY: The Ironworks National Wonder increases a city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production by 8 [ICON_PRODUCTION]. It also provides +1 [ICON_CULTURE] culture. A civilization must have a workshop in all cities before it can construct an Ironworks.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_KREPOST_HELP: +15 XP for all Units. [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_GOLD] Gold costs of acquiring new tiles reduced by 25% in this city.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LABORATORY_HELP: City must have a Public School.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LABORATORY_STRATEGY: The Research Laboratory is a late-game building which greatly increases the [ICON_RESEARCH] Science output of a city. The city must already possess a Public School to construct the Research Laboratory.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LIBRARY_HELP: +1 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science for every 2 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in this City.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LIBRARY_STRATEGY: The Library increases the [ICON_RESEARCH] Science output of the city, speeding up the civ's acquisition of new technology.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LIGHTHOUSE_HELP: +1 [ICON_FOOD] Food from Ocean Tiles.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Each source of [ICON_RES_FISH] Fish worked by this City produces +1 [ICON_FOOD] Food.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built on the coast.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LIGHTHOUSE_STRATEGY: The Lighthouse can only be constructed in a city next to a coastal tile. It increases the [ICON_FOOD] Food output of water tiles and [ICON_RES_FISH] Fish.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LONGHOUSE_HELP: +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production from each worked Forest tile.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LONGHOUSE_PEDIA: For much of their history, many of the Iroquois people lived in villages containing one or more "Longhouses," communal buildings constructed of wood saplings sheathed in elm bark. In fact, the Iroquois call themselves "Hodenosaunee," which translates into "People of the Longhouse." The longhouses ranged from 40 to 400 feet in length and some 25 feet wide. They were divided into compartments by inner partitions, each connected by an open center aisle. Several compartments would share a cooking fire in the center aisle, with an opening in the roof above acting as a chimney.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Longhouses are efficient structures, cheaper to construct and heat than individual dwellings, thus requiring fewer natural resources than other types of homes. They are representative of the Iroquois' cultural desire to survive and thrive in harmony with the environment around them.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_LONGHOUSE_STRATEGY: The Longhouse is the Iroquois unique building, replacing the Workshop. It increases the city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production output for each forest within the city's radius.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MARKET_STRATEGY: The Market significantly increases a city's output of [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MAYA_PYRAMID_STRATEGY: The Pyramid is an Ancient-era building unique to the Maya, replacing the Shrine. It increases the [ICON_RESEARCH] Science and [ICON_PEACE] Faith of a city, speeding Research and the civilization's acquisition of Religions.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MEDICAL_LAB_HELP: 25% of [ICON_FOOD] Food is carried over after a new [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizen is born (effect stacks with Aqueduct).
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MEDICAL_LAB_PEDIA: A medical laboratory is an institution that studies disease and human anatomy using a wide variety of extremely sophisticated and powerful instruments - from ultrasound to cameras the size of an ant to electron microscopes. Most - if not all - of the remarkable advances in medicine in the past 25 years have been made in medical labs. These include the sequencing of human DNA, retro-viruses to battle HIV-AIDS, gene therapy, nano-technology, and more. Much of the increasing cost of healthcare can be attributed to the simple fact that medical laboratories have been remarkably successful at battling illness and prolonging and improving the quality of human life - and these improvements cost huge amounts of money.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MEDICAL_LAB_STRATEGY: The Medical Lab is a late-game building which decreases the amount of [ICON_FOOD] Food a city needs to increase in size by 25%. The city needs to have a Hospital in order to construct the Medical Lab.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MILITARY_ACADEMY_HELP: +15 XP for all Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have an Armory.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MILITARY_ACADEMY_STRATE: The Military Academy is a mid-game building which increases the number of experience points of units constructed in the city. The city must already possess an Armory before the Military Academy can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MILITARY_BASE_STRATEGY: The Military Base is a mid-game building which increases the city's Defensive strength. The city must possess an Arsenal before a Military Base may be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MINT_HELP: Each source of [ICON_RES_GOLD] Gold and [ICON_RES_SILVER] Silver worked by this City produces +2 [ICON_GOLD] Gold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have at least one of these resources mined nearby.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MINT_STRATEGY: The Mint can only be constructed in a city with improved gold or silver resources nearby. The Mint increases the [ICON_GOLD] Gold output of each such resource the city has access to.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MONASTERY_HELP: +2 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture per turn from [ICON_RES_INCENSE] Incense and +2 from [ICON_RES_WINE] Wine nearby.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have an improved source of at least one of these resources nearby.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MONASTERY_STRATEGY: Monasteries increase a city's cultural output if the city is near to improved [ICON_RES_INCENSE] Incense and/or [ICON_RES_WINE] Wine resources. The Monastery cannot be built unless the city has such an improved resource nearby.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MONUMENT_PEDIA: A monument is a structure built to commemorate an important person, event, deity, or concept. The more important monuments are usually constructed near the center of the city, by the ruler's palace, or near the city's main gates. Monuments come in all shapes and sizes, from the Great Sphinx of Giza, Egypt, to the Statue of Liberty in New York City, USA, to Nelson's Column in London, England. The best of them imbue the city's inhabitants with a great feeling of civic and national pride.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MONUMENT_STRATEGY: The Monument increases the [ICON_CULTURE] Culture of a city, speeding the growth of the city's territory and the civilization's acquisition of Social Policies.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MOSQUE_STRATEGY: Can only be built in cities following a religion with the Mosques belief. Construct this building by purchasing it with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUD_PYRAMID_MOSQUE_STRA: The Mud Pyramid Mosque is a Songhai unique building, replacing the Temple. The Mud Pyramid Mosque greatly increases the city's [ICON_CULTURE] Culture, speeding the growth of the city's territory and the civilization's acquisition of Social Policies. The Mud Pyramid Mosque has no maintenance costs.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUGHAL_FORT_HELP: Provides [ICON_GOLD] Gold after Flight is researched.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have Walls.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUGHAL_FORT_PEDIA: The Mughals were an Islamic empire that invaded India in the 15th century, and ruled most of the subcontinent until the mid-19th. During their rule they constructed elaborate forts across the countryside which served as administrative centers and living quarters for the occupiers. These forts were beautiful structures, fitting palaces for the rulers of a vast empire. Several - including the magnificent "Lahore Fort" and Red Fort of Agra - survive today and remain as some of the largest tourist attractions to India, monuments to the Mughals' power and style.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUGHAL_FORT_STRATEGY: The Mughal Fort is a Medieval-era building. It is the Indian unique building, replacing the Castle. The Mughal Fort improves the city's Defensive strength and provides some [ICON_CULTURE] Culture every turn. After Flight is learned, it provides a [ICON_GOLD] Gold bonus to the city as well. Building Walls is a prerequisite for building the Mughal Fort.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUSEUM_STRATEGY: The Museum is a mid-game building which increases the city's [ICON_CULTURE] Culture output. The city must possess an Opera House in order to construct the Museum.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUSICIANS_GUILD_HELP: Provides 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points (GPP) towards a Great Musician. Add up to two specialists to this building to gain [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and increase the rate of Great Musician acquisition (3 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] GPP per specialist).
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_MUSICIANS_GUILD_STRATEG: The Musicians' Guild is the only building that contains musician specialist slots, which are critical for generating Great Musicians. If your goal is a cultural victory, this building is a must-have. The Musicians' Guild provides 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points, and each assigned specialist provides 3 [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great Person Points and 3 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_COLLEGE_HELP: Must have built a Library in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_COLLEGE_PEDIA: A national college is a state-run institute of higher learning. The modern college can be traced back to medieval Islamic madrasah, institutes of law and theology usually associated with mosques. Most countries in the world now have public college and university systems. The University of Tokyo in Japan is one such public institution. According to the Russian Global Universities Ranking, the University of Tokyo is the most prestigious university in Asia and third in the world.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_COLLEGE_STRATE: The National College National Wonder increases a city's [ICON_RESEARCH] Science output by 50%, and it provides +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. A civilization must have a library in all cities before it can construct a National College.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_EPIC_HELP: +25% [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People generation in this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Must have built a Monument in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_EPIC_PEDIA: A national epic is a poem or story which describes the origin of a nation, its character, or a significant event in its existence. The "Epic of Sundiata" is a Malinese national epic. "The Song of Hiawatha" is a United States national epic. The "Kalevala" is an epic of Finland. In a more modern form, "War and Peace" might be considered a Russian national epic, and "Roots: The Saga of an American Family" another American epic.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_EPIC_STRATEGY: This National Wonder increases a city's [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People generation by 25%. It also provides +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. A civilization must have a monument in all cities before it can construct a National Epic.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NATIONAL_TREASURY_HELP: Must have built a Market in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NUCLEAR_PLANT_HELP: Requires 1 [ICON_RES_URANIUM] Uranium. City must not contain a Solar Plant.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_NUCLEAR_PLANT_STRATEGY: The Nuclear Plant significantly increases a city's productivity. A Nuclear Plant requires one [ICON_RES_URANIUM] Uranium resource to be constructed. The city must possess a Factory before it can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OBSERVATORY_HELP: City must be built next to a Mountain.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OBSERVATORY_STRATEGY: The Observatory can only be constructed in a city next to a mountain tile. The Observatory greatly increases the city's [ICON_RESEARCH] Science output.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OPERA_HOUSE_STRATEGY: The Opera House is a Renaissance-era building which increases a city's [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. The city needs to have a Temple or Mud Pyramid Mosque in order to construct the Opera House.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OXFORD_UNIVERSITY_HELP: 1 Free Technology.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Must have built a University in all Cities. The cost goes up the more cities there are in the empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OXFORD_UNIVERSITY_PEDIA: Oxford University is the oldest university in England, and in all of the English-speaking world. Founded sometime late in the 11th century, Oxford has been in continuous operation ever since. Famous Oxford graduates include Roger Bacon, William of Ockham, Sir Walter Raleigh, John Donne, Jonathan Swift, Dr. Samuel Johnson, Oscar Wilde, Sir Richard Burton, and J.R.R. Tolkien.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_OXFORD_UNIVERSITY_STRAT: The Oxford University National Wonder provides 1 free technology and +1 [ICON_CULTURE] Culture. The civilization must have a university in all cities before it can construct Oxford University.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PAGODA_STRATEGY: Can only be built in cities following a religion with the Pagodas belief. Construct this building by purchasing it with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PALACE_HELP: Indicates this City is the [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital of the empire.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][ICON_CONNECTED] Connecting other Cities to the [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital by Road will produce additional [ICON_GOLD] Gold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]+3 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production, +3 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science, +3 [ICON_GOLD] Gold
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PALACE_STRATEGY: Your palace is the seat of your government. Early in the game it provides a significant portion of your capital city's culture, gold, science, and production. It also forms the heart of your empire's trading system. Although the palace is automatically rebuilt in another city if your capital is captured, it can still be a terrible blow to your civilization (and it makes it impossible for you to win a Domination victory until you get it back). Protect your palace!
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PAPER_MAKER_HELP: +1 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science for every 2 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in this City. +2[ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PAPER_MAKER_PEDIA: Paper was invented in China and dates back as far as 200 BC. As best as can be determined, it was originally used as a packing material, with the earliest surviving example of writing on paper dating to perhaps 8 BC. By 300 AD writing paper was in widespread use across China. The Chinese Paper Maker workshops were highly advanced for their day, creating clean, sturdy paper for the Chinese educated classes, merchants, and the Imperial Court centuries before the first sheet reached the Arabic and European world.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PAPER_MAKER_STRATEGY: The Paper Maker is the Chinese unique building, replacing the Library. The Paper Maker increases the city's acquisition of [ICON_RESEARCH] Science and also provides a boost to the city's intake of [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PARTHENON_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"Earth proudly wears the Parthenon as the best gem upon her zone."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Ralph Waldo Emerson[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_POLICE_STATION_HELP: Reduces enemy spy stealing rate by 25%.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a Constabulary.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_POLICE_STATION_STRATEGY: Police stations slow down the rate at which enemy spies can steal technologies from your city. The city must possess an Constabulary in order to construct the Police Station.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PRORA_RESORT_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"This loss (of freedom) means the fading from human life of values infinitely precious to it. There only remain ironbound conditions of employment and trivial amusements for leisure."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - A.N. Whitehead[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PUBLIC_SCHOOL_HELP: +1 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science for every 2 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in this City.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a University.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_PUBLIC_SCHOOL_STRATEGY: The Public School is a mid-game building which greatly increases a city's output of [ICON_RESEARCH] Science. The city must have already constructed a university or wat in order to build a Public School.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_RECYCLING_CENTER_HELP: Provides 2 [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum. Maximum of 5 of these buildings in your empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_RECYCLING_CENTER_STRATE: Each Recycling Center provides 2 [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum. Maximum of 5 of these buildings in your empire.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_RED_FORT_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"When we build, let us think that we build forever."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - John Ruskin[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ROYAL_LIBRARY_HELP: Unique Assyrian Library replacement. +1 [ICON_RESEARCH] Science for every 2 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in this City. Also has a slot for a Great Work of Writing which, when filled, gives extra XP to trained Units.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_ROYAL_LIBRARY_STRATEGY: In addition to the [ICON_RESEARCH] Science output of the Library it replaces, the Royal Library also has a Great Work of Writing slot. Once filled with a Great Work, it provides extra XP to Units trained in this city, so place a Great Work here as soon as possible for the maximum benefit. Only the Assyrians may build it.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SATRAPS_COURT_PEDIA: A "satrap" is the title of a provisional governor in the Achaemenian Empire of Persia. The satraps were appointed by the king, and were usually members of the royal family. A powerful figure, the satrap collected taxes and was the supreme judicial figure in the province. He was responsible for raising and maintaining an army, and therefore had to be highly trusted by the monarch. As the central power of the Persian empire weakened during the 5th century BC, the Satraps grew more independent, and some were in fact more powerful than the king they allegedly answered to.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Alexander maintained the satrapal administration after he conquered Persia, sometimes replacing the Satraps with his generals and sometimes retaining the existing governors if they maintained the peace and kept paying their taxes.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SATRAPS_COURT_STRATEGY: The Satrap's Court is the Persian unique building. It replaces the Bank. Like a bank the Satrap's Court increases the city's output of wealth. However, the Satrap's Court also increases the civilization's [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness. The city must have a Market before the Satrap's Court may be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SEAPORT_HELP: +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production and [ICON_GOLD] Gold from Sea Resources worked by this City. +15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Naval Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must be built on the coast.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SEAPORT_STRATEGY: A Seaport increases the [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production output of an improved sea resource.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SHRINE_STRATEGY: The Shrine is the earliest building that increases the [ICON_PEACE] Faith generated by a city, allowing you to found and spread religions.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SOLAR_PLANT_HELP: City must be built on or next to Desert and must not contain a Nuclear Plant.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a Factory.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SOLAR_PLANT_STRATEGY: A Solar Plant is a late-game building which significantly increases a city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production. The city must be constructed on or adjacent to a desert tile in order to build the Solar Plant. The city must possess a Factory before it can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SPACESHIP_FACTORY_HELP: +50% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Spaceship Parts. Requires 1 [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have a Factory.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SPACESHIP_FACTORY_STRAT: The Spaceship Factory increases the speed at which a city constructs spaceship parts. The Spaceship Factory requires one [ICON_RES_ALUMINUM] Aluminum resource, and the city must possess a Factory before it can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STABLE_HELP: +15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Mounted Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Each source of [ICON_RES_HORSE] Horses [ICON_RES_SHEEP] Sheep and [ICON_RES_COW] Cattle worked by this City produce +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have at least one of these resources improved with a Pasture.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STABLE_STRATEGY: The Stable can only be constructed in a city near to one or more improved [ICON_RES_HORSE] horse, [ICON_RES_SHEEP] sheep or [ICON_RES_COW] cattle resources. The Stable increases the speed of production of mounted units.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STADIUM_HELP: It cannot provide more [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness than there are [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in a city. City must have a Theatre.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STADIUM_STRATEGY: A Stadium is a Modern-era building which increases the [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness of your entire empire, not just the city in which it is constructed. The city must already possess a Theatre before it can construct a Stadium.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STELE_STRATEGY: The Stele is an Ancient-era building unique to Ethiopia, replacing the Monument. It increases the [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_PEACE] Faith of a city, speeding the growth of the city's territory and the civilization's acquisition of Social Policies and Religions.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STONE_WORKS_HELP: Each source of [ICON_RES_MARBLE] Marble and [ICON_RES_STONE] Stone worked by this City produce +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must have at least one of these resources improved with a Quarry.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]City must not be in Plains.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_STONE_WORKS_STRATEGY: Stone Works can only be constructed in a city near an improved [ICON_RES_STONE] Stone or [ICON_RES_MARBLE] Marble resource. Stone Works increase a city's [ICON_PRODUCTION] production. The city must not be in Plains.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_SYDNEY_OPERA_HOUSE_PEDI: The Sydney Opera House is one of the grandest modern opera houses in the world. Constructed over a 20-year period ending in 1973, the Sydney Opera House is a beautiful building sitting atop Bennelong Point alongside Sydney Harbor in Sydney, Australia. Designed as a series of shells, or wings, the structure is one of the most recognizable in the world and it has come to symbolize Australia itself, in much the same way that the Statue of Liberty symbolizes the United States or the Great Pyramids symbolize Egypt. It houses an Opera Theatre, Concert Hall, three smaller venues, and a restaurant.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_TEMPLE_STRATEGY: The Temple increases the Culture of a city, speeding the growth of the city's territory and the civilization's acquisition of Social Policies. The city must contain a Monument before the Temple can be constructed.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_THEATRE_HELP: It cannot provide more [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness than there are [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in a city. City must have a Colosseum.
TXT_KEY_BUILDING_THEATRE_PEDIA: The Greeks invented theatre as it's known in the West today. The original theatres were open-air amphitheatres often constructed on the side of a hill. The audience sat on benches cut into the hill, while the performers worked on an open stage at the foot of the hill. Over time the stages moved indoors (especially in locations with risks of lots of inclement weather).
