The fallout "falls out" of the air as a layer of radioactive particles which are highly dangerous to plants and animals, killing them immediately or damaging their DNA, giving them cancer, other diseases, or unfortunate mutations. Depending upon the type of nuclear explosion, the land may remain poisoned for decades, possibly centuries. Cleanup requires the replacement of the contaminated buildings, soil and vegetation.
TXT_KEY_FEATURE_ICE_PEDIA: Ice is impassable, except to air and submarine units. It provides nothing to cities.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_ADV_QUEST: What is the fog of war?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_FOG_ADV_QUEST: What does it mean when a tile is under the fog of war?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_FOG_HEADING3_BODY: Tiles under the clouds of the fog of war are totally unknown to you. You don't know what kind of terrain they are, who occupies them, or anything else. For all you know they may be flowing with gold or patrolled by ninja dinosaurs. You'd better get someone out to explore them as quickly as possible!
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_FOG_HEADING3_TITLE: Fog of War on a Tile
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_HEADING1_BODY: The world is a big place, and you don't always know what's going on everywhere. Early civilizations certainly didn't have any idea what the people on the other side of the mountains looked like until they sent out explorers; unless they put out sentries, they might not know that an enemy was massing a huge army just outside of their own borders. In Civilization V, until you explore the world, it's hidden in the "fog of war."[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]The fog of war is represented by the white clouds that cover much of the world at the start of the game. As you move units around, the fog of war will pull back, revealing more of the world. Once you have uncovered the fog of war from a tile, it doesn't come back. However, if a unit moves and you can no longer see a tile, you won't know if anything is going on there.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_INDIRECTFIRE_HEADING2_B: Some ranged units are capable of "indirect fire," which means that they can shoot over obstacles like forests or hills, which normally block a ranged unit's attack, as long as another unit can "see" the target.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_OBSCURINGTERRAIN_HEADIN: Mountains and Natural Wonders are impenetrable: they totally bar visibility of what's beyond for everything (except for flying units).[NEWLINE]Forests, mountains and hills are all "blocking" terrain. Units can see into such tiles, but they cannot see past them - unless they occupy a hill. Units in hills can see over blocking terrain into the tiles beyond.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_REVEALED_ADV_QUEST: What does it mean when a tile is revealed?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_REVEALED_HEADING3_BODY: If you have uncovered the fog of war from a tile but cannot see it at the present moment (because the exploring unit has moved away, for instance), the tile is slightly darkened. You can still see the terrain in the tile, but you will not see any units in the tile. You may not see any improvements, recently-constructed cities, and so forth. Basically, your information about that tile may be well out of date.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_SEEN_ADV_QUEST: What is visible to a player?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_SEEN_HEADING2_BODY: You can always see everything within your borders, as well as one tile away from your borders. Most units can see everything within 2 tiles (except for tiles behind mountains and blocking tiles). Units on hills can see over blocked tiles. Certain promotions will extend a unit's sight by 1 tile, and also, a number of mid- to late-game naval units have extended sight as well.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_THREESTATES_ADV_QUEST: What are the states of visibility?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_THREESTATES_HEADING2_BO: The three states of tile visibility are visible, revealed, and fog of war.
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_VISIBLE_ADV_QUEST: What does it mean when a tile is visible?
TXT_KEY_FOGOFWAR_VISIBLE_HEADING3_BODY: If a tile is currently visible to a unit or your territory, you can see its terrain, any improvements on it, if it's within any borders, whether it's part of a city, any unit which may occupy it, and so forth. Subject to technology limitations, you'll see the resources in the tile as well.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_ADV_QUEST: How does food effect city growth?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BESTTILES_ADV_QUEST: Which tiles provide the most food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BESTTILES_HEADING3_BODY: The tile types that provide a lot of food include Oasis, Floodplains, and Grassland.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BONUS_ADV_QUEST: How can bonus resources provide more food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BONUS_HEADING4_BODY: Tiles with "bonus" resources provide a lot of food once a worker constructs the appropriate improvement on the resource. These include bananas, cattle, deer, fish, sheep, and wheat. See the sections on workers and on resources for more details.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BUCKET_ADV_QUEST: What is this "city growth bucket" and why should I care?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BUCKET_HEADING3_BODY: The city growth bucket contains all of the excess food produced by a city each turn. When the quantity of food reaches a specific amount, the city's population (citizens) will increase by 1; then the city growth bucket is emptied and the process begins all over again. The amount of food needed for population growth increases significantly as the city gets bigger.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]The City Info Box in the upper left-hand corner of the City Screen tells you how many turns until the city grows; and the "Food" entry in that box tells you how much food the city is currently producing each turn. Hover your cursor over the "Food" entry to see exactly how much food you need to fill the City Growth Bucket.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BUILDINGWONDERSOCIAL_ADV_QU: How can buildings, wonders, and social policies effect city growth?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_BUILDINGWONDERSOCIAL_HEADIN: Certain buildings, wonders and social policies will affect the amount of food a city produces or how much it needs in its "bucket" to grow.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_CITIES_ADV_QUEST: How much food is needed for a city to grow?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_CITIES_HEADING2_BODY: A city requires 2 food per citizen (another term for "population") per turn to avoid starvation. A city acquires food (as well as production and gold) by assigning its citizens to "work" the land around the city. The city can work any tile within three spaces of the city that is also within the civilization's borders, provided as well that it is not being worked by another city.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Left to its own devices, the city will assign as many citizens as needed to acquire its food. If not enough is available, the city will starve, losing citizens until it can support itself.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_CITYGROWTH_HEADING2_BODY: Each turn, a city's citizens gathers a certain amount of food from the land around it and from various other sources as described above. The city's citizens have first call on that food, and they consume 2 food for each population point (so a city of population 7 consumes 14 food each turn). Any food left over is put into the poetically-named "City Growth Bucket."
TXT_KEY_FOOD_FLOODPLAINS_ADV_QUEST: Do flood plains provide a lot of food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_FLOODPLAINS_HEADING4_BODY: Flood plains provide a lot of food, particularly if improved with a farm.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_GETFOOD_ADV_QUEST: How can you get more food for a city?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_GETFOOD_HEADING2_BODY: Certain tiles provide more food than others, and cities near to one or more of these tiles will grow faster. In addition, workers can "improve" many tiles with farms, increasing their output of food.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_GRASSLAND_ADV_QUEST: Do grasslands and jungle tiles provide a good amount of food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_GRASSLAND_HEADING4_BODY: These tiles also provide a good amount of food.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_HEADING1_BODY: Plentiful food is the single most important factor determining the rise of human civilization. While humans had to spend virtually every waking moment hunting and gathering food for themselves and their families or tribe, they had little time or energy for other pursuits - making cave paintings, for instance, creating a written language, or discovering ruins. Once surplus food is available, then all else is possible.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_IMPROVEMENTS_ADV_QUEST: How can you improve a tile's food output?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_IMPROVEMENTS_HEADING3_BODY: Workers can construct farms on most tiles to improve their food output.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_MARITIME_ADV_QUEST: How can maritime city-states effect your food intake?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_MARITIME_HEADING3_BODY: If you befriend a maritime city-state, it will provide food to all of your cities, with your capital getting the largest portion of the food.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_OASIS_ADV_QUEST: Do oases produce a lot of food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_OASIS_HEADING4_BODY: Oasis provide a lot of food, particularly when compared with the desert in which they're usually found.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_SETTLERS_ADV_QUEST: How does building settlers effect a city's food use?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_SETTLERS_HEADING2_BODY: Settlers can only be constructed in cities of size 2 or larger. During construction, settlers consume a city's production and all of the city's excess food intake. As long as the settler is in production, the city will not grow or add food to its growth bucket. (Note that settlers don't subtract food from the bucket; they consume excess food being produced, stopping more food from going into that bucket.) See the section on Settlers for more details.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_UNHAPPINESS_HEADING3_BODY: If your civilization is unhappy, then the city will produce less food. The city will produce enough food to feed its citizens, but there is a 75% decrease in the amount of food it produces.
TXT_KEY_FOOD_WELOVEKING_ADV_QUEST: How does "We Love the King Day" effect food?
TXT_KEY_FOOD_WELOVEKING_HEADING3_BODY: If a city goes into "We Love the King Day" (see the section on Resources) its surplus food input increases by 25%. (If no surplus, no benefit.)
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_ALWAYS_WAR_HELP: Players start at war with one another and may not go to peace.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_COMPLETE_KILLS_HELP: In order to be eliminated from the game, a player must have all of his Cities AND Units destroyed.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_DISABLE_START_BIAS_H: Disables matching Civilizations to certain map conditions. Starts will be totally random.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_DYNAMIC_TURNS_HELP: Players take turns based on their war status.[NEWLINE]Warring human players take their turns sequentially.[NEWLINE]Non-warring players take their turns simultaneously.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_END_TURN_TIMER_ENABL: Turns will end automatically when timer finishes.[NEWLINE]0 = Game dynamically scales the turn time.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_LOCK_MODS_HELP: Prevents this game from being loaded while mods are active. Useful for competitive games.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NEW_RANDOM_SEED_HELP: Each time the game is loaded, the random number seed is regenerated. This means if you attack after loading, you might get a different result from the first time you played.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_BARBARIANS_HELP: Barbarians and their Encampments do not appear on the map.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_CHANGING_WAR_PEAC: Whatever the state of war or peace the game begins with, the players may not change once the game is underway. Useful for mods, like World War II.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_CITY_RAZING_HELP: Players are not allowed to burn cities down after capturing them.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_CULTURE_OVERVIEW_: Disables the Culture Overview Screen. This affects only the UI and does not alter any of the core game mechanics.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_ESPIONAGE_HELP: Disables espionage system. No spies are granted.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_GOODY_HUTS_HELP: Ancient Ruins do not appear on the map.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_HAPPINESS_HELP: No penalties or benefits from happiness.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_LEAGUES_HELP: Disables the World Congress system. No proposals or sessions will happen, and Diplomatic Victory will not be possible.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_POLICIES_HELP: Disables policy system. No policies granted in the game.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_RELIGION_HELP: Disables religion system. No Faith earned. Pantheons and Religions may not be founded.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_SCIENCE_HELP: Disables research system. Civilizations are locked at the starting technology level.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_NO_TUTORIAL_HELP: Disables the tutorial popups for the duration of the game.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_ONE_CITY_CHALLENGE_H: HUMAN players are only ever allowed to build or own one city.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_PITBOSS_HELP: Players (other than the host) can enter/exit the game whenever they want. Players are sent turn notifications when it is their turn.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_POLICY_SAVING_HELP: Players may choose to defer selecting a Social Policy until later, instead of needing to pick immediately.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_PROMOTION_SAVING_HEL: Players may choose to defer selecting a Unit Promotion until later, instead of needing to pick immediately.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_QUICK_COMBAT_HELP: No combat animations are played when units attack.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_QUICK_MOVEMENT_HELP: No animations are played when units move.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_RAGING_BARBARIANS_HE: The rate at which Barbarians spawn is greatly increased.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_RANDOM_PERSONALITIES: Instead of acting as they normally do, a random personality will be picked for each CPU leader, making the game more unpredictable.
TXT_KEY_GAME_OPTION_SIMULTANEOUS_TURNS_H: Players take their turns at the same time.
TXT_KEY_GAMESPEED_EPIC_HELP: Production, Research, etc. all take a fair amount longer than when playing on Standard Speed. The game is balanced to take longer but still feel like a normal game.
TXT_KEY_GAMESPEED_MARATHON_HELP: This extremely long game speed will allow almost an entire normal game's worth of turns for each era.
TXT_KEY_GAMESPEED_QUICK_HELP: The number of turns required to complete various tasks is compressed. Good for players who don't like to wait around too much.
TXT_KEY_GLOBE_THEATER_THEMING_BONUS_HELP: To maximize your bonus, make sure the Great Work Slots are all filled with Writings from the same Era and Civilization.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_ADV_QUEST: What is gold and what can I do with it?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_ANCIENTRUINS_ADV_QUEST: Can ancient ruins give you more gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_ANCIENTRUINS_HEADING3_BODY: An ancient ruin may provide gold when it is explored.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BARBARIAN_ADV_QUEST: How can I get gold from a barbarian encampment?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BARBARIAN_HEADING3_BODY: You'll earn gold each time you disperse a Barbarian Encampment.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BUILDINGS_ADV_QUEST: Which buildings increase the output of gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BUILDINGS_HEADING3_BODY: Many buildings - markets, banks - increase a city's output of gold, especially if you assign merchant specialists to them.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BUYFRIENDSHIP_ADV_QUEST: How can I use gold to buy influence with city-states?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_BUYFRIENDSHIP_HEADING3_BODY: If you want to improve your relationship with a city-state, one way to do so is to give it some gold. Increasing amounts of gold may be given for larger boosts to friendship.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_CAPTURE_ADV_QUEST: Do you get gold from capturing a city?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_CAPTURE_HEADING3_BODY: You may gain a bunch of gold when you capture a city (city-state or civilization's possession).
TXT_KEY_GOLD_CITYSTATES_ADV_QUEST: How can you get gold out of a city state?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_CITYSTATES_HEADING3_BODY: A city-state may give you gold when you first meet. It may provide more later if you befriend it. (See the section on City-States for details.)
TXT_KEY_GOLD_DIPLOMACY_ADV_QUEST: How can you gain gold through diplomacy?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_DIPLOMACY_HEADING3_BODY: You may gain gold - lump sum or an amount each turn for 30 turns - during negotiations with another civ.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_EXPENDING_ADV_QUEST: What can I spend gold on?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_EXPENDING_DIPLOMACY_ADV_QUE: How can I use gold in diplomacy?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_EXPENDING_DIPLOMACY_HEADING: You can exchange gold with other civilizations for any number of reasons - trading it for resources, for example, to get the other civ to make peace with you, or to bribe the civ to attack a third. Gold is extremely useful in negotiations.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]There are two different ways to exchange gold: flat fee and per turn.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_EXPENDING_HEADING2_BODY: There's lots of stuff to spend gold on.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_FLATFEE_ADV_QUEST: What is a "flat fee" gold exchange in diplomacy?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_FLATFEE_HEADING4_BODY: A "Flat Fee" exchange is just that. You give or receive a one-time lump sum of gold, and then you're done.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_HEADING1_BODY: Ah, Gold! Gold is wonderful stuff. You can use it to build an army, to pay for a road network, to purchase buildings, to buy the friendship of a city-state and to bribe an enemy civilization.[NEWLINE]It may be true that "money can't buy you love," but it can purchase a nuclear submarine armed with nuclear missiles, and that's not bad.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_LOSECITY_ADV_QUEST: Do you lose gold if your city is captured?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_LOSECITY_HEADING3_BODY: If a civilization or city-state captures one of your cities, they take some of your gold (as well as the city).
TXT_KEY_GOLD_MAINTENANCE_ADV_QUEST: How does gold work with building and unit maintenance costs?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_MAINTENANCE_HEADING3_BODY: Units and buildings both have "maintenance costs" that must be paid every turn. See the individual entries on the units and buildings for specific amounts. (Note that these maintenance costs are dependent upon the difficulty level at which you're playing.)
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PERTURN_ADV_QUEST: What is a "per turn" gold exchange in diplomacy?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PERTURN_HEADING4_BODY: You can also negotiate an exchange that occurs over a number of turns (usually 30). For example, you might agree to pay the other civ 5 gold per turn for 30 turns. These agreements are rendered null and void if the two civilizations go to war.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PILLAGE_ADV_QUEST: Does pillaging improvements provide any gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PILLAGE_HEADING3_BODY: Pillaging enemy improvements will give you a modest amount of gold.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PLUNDERED_ADV_QUEST: Do you lose gold if barbarians take your city?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PLUNDERED_HEADING3_BODY: If the fiendish barbarians successfully attack one of your cities, they "plunder" some of your gold and you retain the city.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PURCHASETILES_ADV_QUEST: How can you expand your borders using gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PURCHASETILES_HEADING3_BODY: You can extend your civilization's territory by purchasing individual tiles. Go to a City Screen, then click on "Buy a Tile." The map will display all tiles available for purchase. Click on the tile to expend the requisite gold and purchase the tile.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PURCHASEUNITS_ADV_QUEST: How can you use gold to units, buildings, or wonders?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_PURCHASEUNITS_HEADING3_BODY: You can spend gold to purchase units or buildings in a city. Click on an item (if you can afford it!) and it will be immediately constructed in the city, and the amount deducted from your treasury.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Note that "projects" - the Utopia Project, the Manhattan Project, etc. - cannot be purchased.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_RESOURCES_ADV_QUEST: Can I get gold from resources?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_RESOURCES_HEADING3_BODY: All Luxury resources (especially gold!) provide gold when worked.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_ROAD_ADV_QUEST: How does gold work with road maintenance?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_ROAD_HEADING3_BODY: You spend gold for each road tile that you construct. If you absorb another civ's roads into your territory, you pay for their maintenance as well.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_RUNNINGOUT_ADV_QUEST: What happens if you run out of gold and are running a negative budget?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_RUNNINGOUT_HEADING2_BODY: If your treasury is at zero and you're running a negative budget, the difference is deducted from your Science, and can eventually cause units to be disbanded. Beware: this can seriously slow down your acquisition of new technology, which can leave you extremely vulnerable to attack by more advanced neighbors. Get your budget in order as quickly as possible!
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TERRAIN_ADV_QUEST: How can I get gold from the terrain?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TERRAIN_HEADING3_BODY: These tiles provide gold when your citizens work them:[NEWLINE]- Coast Tiles[NEWLINE]- Ocean Tiles[NEWLINE]- River Tiles[NEWLINE]- Natural Wonders[NEWLINE]- Oasis
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADE_ROUTES_ADV_QUEST: How does a trade route produce gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADE_ROUTES_BLOCKADE_ADV_Q: How is a naval trade route blockaded?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADE_ROUTES_BLOCKADE_HEADI: An enemy naval unit within 2 tiles of a port city will "blockade" that city, rendering its harbor trade route inoperative until the enemy unit is driven off or destroyed. Citizens cannot work naval tiles that are blockaded.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADE_ROUTES_HEADING3_BODY: If a city is connected by a road and/or harbor to your capital city, that city has a "trade route" with the capital. Each trade route is worth a certain amount of gold each turn, the amount determined by the size of the two cities.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADEMISSION_ADV_QUEST: How can you use the great merchant to get lots of gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADEMISSION_HEADING3_BODY: A Great Merchant can perform a "trade mission" in a city-state. The Merchant is expended and you get lots of gold. (See the section on Great People.)
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADINGPOST_ADV_QUEST: Does the trading post provide gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_TRADINGPOST_HEADING3_BODY: Construct a trading post improvement in a tile to increase its gold output.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_UPGRADE_UNITS_ADV_QUEST: A unit must be in your territory to be upgraded.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_UPGRADE_UNITS_HEADING3_BODY: Over time, you'll learn new technologies that will allow you to create better military units than those you previously could. When this occurs, you'll have the option to "upgrade" the older units, turning them into the newer, more powerful models. (For example, once you learn Iron Working, you can upgrade any Warrior units you possess into Swordsmen.) Each upgrade costs some gold - the more powerful the upgrade, the more expensive it will be.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]A unit must be in your territory to be upgraded. When an upgrade is available for a unit, the "Upgrade" button will appear in the unit's Action list.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_WHERETOGET_ADV_QUEST: Where can I get more gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_WHERETOGET_HEADING2_BODY: Gold comes from a variety of sources. You'll get most of your gold by working the tiles around your cities, but other sources are available as well.
TXT_KEY_GOLD_WONDERS_ADV_QUEST: How do wonders interact with gold?
TXT_KEY_GOLD_WONDERS_HEADING3_BODY: Some Wonders provide or increase a city's output of gold. Check out Machu Picchu and the Colossus.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Also, if you're constructing a Wonder and another civ finishes it before you do, you get a gold bonus (the size of the bonus depends upon how much progress you've made on the Wonder).
TXT_KEY_GOODY_BARBARIANS: Lying in wait are treacherous Barbarians!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_BARBARIANS: Tell these people that they are barbarians. Repeatedly.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_CULTURE: Use this contact with the lost tribe to enhance your [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_EXPERIENCE: Study with the lost tribe to have your unit gain experience.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_GOLD: Trade with the lost tribe for [ICON_GOLD] Gold.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_HEALING: Drink the native potions to heal your unit.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_MAP: Have a look at their maps.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_POPULATION: Convince the remaining population to join one of your cities.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_REVEAL_NEARBY_BARBS: Use information from the tribe to locate nearby barbarian camps.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_REVEAL_RESOURCE: Ask for directions to a nearby reseource.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_SCOUT: Convince the lost tribe to become a Scout for your civilization.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_SETTLER: Convince the lost tribe to become a Settler for your civilization.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_TECH: Study their tools to discover a new technology.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_UPGRADE_UNIT: Use equipment around the tribe to upgrade your unit.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_WARRIOR: Recruit a Warrior from the lost tribe.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CHOOSE_WORKER: Convince the lost tribe to become a Worker for your civilization.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_CULTURE: You have found cultural artifacts which awe your citizens! You have received {1_Num} [ICON_CULTURE] Culture!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_EXPERIENCE: Exploring the ruins reveals ancient scriptures which provide your Unit new combat training!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_FAITH: You have found an ancient oracle. The priestess has blessed you with a divine revelation. You have received {1_Num} [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_GOLD: In the ruins you find the treasures of an ancient empire! You have received {1_Num} [ICON_GOLD] Gold!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_HEALING: Amongst the ruins you find ancient medicines which your Unit uses to heal its wounded.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_MAP: You discover a crudely-drawn map which outlines the surrounding area!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_POPULATION: You find survivors lost in the ruins! In gratitude they settle in one of your Cities, increasing its [ICON_CITIZEN] population!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_PROPHET_FAITH: You have found a secret religious community. The preacher foretells the arrival of a Great Prophet. You have received {1_Num} [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_GOODY_REVEAL_NEARBY_BARBS: Evidence of recent activity reveals the location of nearby Barbarian Encampments!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_REVEAL_RESOURCE: You find crudely-drawn maps which lead you to the location of an unknown Resource! (You will still need to learn the proper Technology to utilize the new resource.)
TXT_KEY_GOODY_SCOUT: You find survivors among the ruins, which agree to join your empire as Scouts!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_SETTLER: You find survivors among the ruins, which agree to join your empire as Settlers!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_TECH: Discovered in the ruins are the secrets of an advanced technology!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_UPGRADE_UNIT: Your Unit equips itself with advanced weapons found in the ruins!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_WARRIOR: You find a group of Warriors willing to join your civilization!
TXT_KEY_GOODY_WORKER: You find survivors among the ruins, which agree to join your empire as Workers!
TXT_KEY_GREAT_LIBRARY_THEMING_BONUS_HELP: To maximize your bonus, make sure the Great Work Slots are all filled with Writings from different Eras and different Civilizations.
TXT_KEY_GREAT_PERSON_WILLEM_THE_YOUNGER: Willem van de Velde the Younger
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_A_LA_RECHERCHE: À la recherche du temps perdu (In Search of Lost Time)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_A_THOUSAND_LI: A Thousand Li of Rivers and Mountains
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_ALONG_THE_RIVER: Along the River During Qingming Festival
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_ARRANGEMENT_IN_GREY: Arrangement in Grey and Black: Portrait of the Artist's Mother
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_AT_THE_EDGE_OF_THE_BR: At the Edge of the Brook
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_ATTACK_ON_BEIJING: An attack on Beijing Castle during the Boxer Rebellion
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_BRANDENBERG_CONCERTOS: Brandenburg Concertos (Concerto III in G Major)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_CEREMONIAL_SITTING: Ceremonial Sitting of the State Council on 7 May 1901
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_DAUD_RECEIVES_A_ROBE: Da'ud Receives a Robe of Honor from Mun'im Khan
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_DUTCH_MEN_O_WAR: Dutch men-o'-war and other shipping in a calm
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_EMPEROR_TAIZONG: Emperor Taizong Receiving the Tibetan Envoy
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_EVENING_SHOWER: Evening Shower at Atake and the Great Bridge
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_FROM_THE_NEW_WORLD: From the New World (Symphone No. 9 in E Minor)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_FRONT_OF_THE_MIRROR: Naked Young Woman in Front of the Mirror
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_GIANT_MAGNOLIAS: Giant Magnolias on a Blue Velvet Cloth
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_HARBOR_IN_BALAKLAVA: Embarkation of sick persons at the harbor in Balaklava
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_HONG_LOU_MENG: Hong Lou Meng (Dream of the Red Chamber)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_JANE_GREY: The Execution of Lady Jane Grey
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_JIN_PING_MEI: Jin Ping Mei (The Plum in the Gold Vase)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_LA_VIE_DE_GARGANTUA: La Vie de Gargantua et de Pantagruel
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_LE_MORTE_DARTHUR: Le Morte D'Arthur (The Death of Arthur)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_MEXICAN_EXPEDITION: Scene from the Mexican Expedition in 1838
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_PARTHENON_FRIEZE: Phidias Showing the Frieze of the Parthenon to his Friends (Lawrence Alma-Tadema)
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_PORTRAIT_OF_A_MAN: Portrait of a Man in a Turban
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_PORTRAIT_OF_NASSER: Portrait of Nasser al-Din Shah Qajar
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_A_CHRISTMAS_CAR: "You may be an undigested bit of beef, a blot of mustard, a crumb of cheese, a fragment of underdone potato. There's more of gravy than of grave about you, whatever you are!"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_A_LA_RECHERCHE: "There is no man, however wise, who has not at some point in his youth said things, or lived a life, the memory of which is so unpleasant to him that he would gladly expunge it. And yet he ought not entirely regret it, because he cannot be certain that he has indeed become a wise man."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ABHIJNANASAKUTA: "Yesterday is but a dream;[NEWLINE]Tomorrow is only a vision. But Today well lived makes every[NEWLINE]yesterday a dream of happiness;[NEWLINE]and every tomorrow a vision of hope."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ADVENTURES_OF_H: "But I reckon I got to light out for the Territory ahead of the rest, because Aunt Sally she's going to adopt me and sivilize me, and I can't stand it. I been there before."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_AENEID: "It is easy to go down into Hell;[NEWLINE]night and day the gates of dark Death stand wide;[NEWLINE]but to climb back again, to retrace one's steps to the upper air -[NEWLINE]there's the rub, the task."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ALICES_ADVANTUR: "'But I don't want to go among mad people,' Alice remarked.[NEWLINE]'Oh, you can't help that,' said the Cat. 'We're all mad here. I'm mad. You're mad.'[NEWLINE]'How do you know I'm mad?' said Alice.[NEWLINE]'You must be,' said the Cat, 'or you wouldn't have come here.'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_AZUL: "Bagpipes of Spain, ye that can sing[NEWLINE]That which is sweetest to us in the Spring![NEWLINE]You first sing of gladness and then sing of pain[NEWLINE]As deep and as bitter as the billowed main."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_BAKHSHALI: "Five merchants together buy a jewel. Its price is equal to half the money possessed by the first together with the money possessed by the others, or one-third of the money possessed by the second together with the moneys possessed by the others..."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_BARRACK_ROOM_BA: "And the first rude sketch that the world has seen[NEWLINE]was joy to his heart.[NEWLINE]Till the Devil whispered behind the leaves,[NEWLINE]'It's pretty, but is it art?'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CANDIDE_OU_LOPT: "Fools have a habit of believing that everything written by a famous author is admirable. For my part I read only to please myself and only what suits my taste."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CANTERBURY_TALE: "Yet do not miss the moral, my good men.[NEWLINE]For Saint Paul says that all that's written well[NEWLINE]Is written down some useful truth to tell.[NEWLINE]Then take the wheat and let the chaff lie still."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CHAYKA: "Why do I hear a note of sadness that wrings my heart in this cry of a pure soul? If at any time you should have need of my life, come and take it."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CHU_CI: "Two monarchs then, who high renown received,[NEWLINE]followed the kingly way, their goal achieved.[NEWLINE]Two princes proud by lust their reign abused,[NEWLINE]sought easier path, and their own steps confused."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CODEX_BORGIA: "Tezcatlipoca: He by whom we live. Lord of the Near and the Nigh."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_CODEX_PEREZ: "The prophecy of Chilam Balam, the singer, of Cabal-chen, Mani. On 13 Ahau the katun will end in the time of the Itza, in the time of Tancah"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_DEAD_SEA: "Tell them this, 'These gods, who did not make the heavens and the earth, will perish from the earth and from under the heavens.'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_DIE_RAUBER: "Not that I have any doubt but that justice would find me speedily enough if the powers above so ordained it. But she might surprise me in sleep, or overtake me in flight, or seize me with violence and the sword, and then I should have lost the only merit left me, that of making my death a free-will atonement."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_DIVINA_COMMEDIA: "The hottest places in Hell are reserved for those who, in times of great moral crisis, maintain their neutrality."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_EL_INGENIOSO: "'Now look, your grace,' said Sancho, 'what you see over there are not giants, but windmills, and what seems to be arms are just sails, that go around in the wind and turn the millstone.'[NEWLINE]'Obviously,' replied Don Quixote, 'you don't know much about adventures.'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_FABLES: "The tortoise plodded on and on. When the Hare awoke from his nap, he saw the Tortoise near the winning post. But he could not run fast enough in time to save the race. Then the Tortoise said, 'Slow and steady wins the race.'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_FAUST: "When scholars study a thing, they strive to kill it first, if it's alive; then they have the parts and the'be lost the whole, for the link that's missing was the living soul."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_FRANKENSTEIN: "Learn from me, if not by my precepts, at least by my example, how dangerous is the acquirement of knowledge, and how much happier that man is who believes his native town to be the world, than he who aspires to become greater than his nature will allow."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_GENJI_MONOGATAR: "One ought not to be unkind to a woman merely on account of her plainness, any more than one had a right to take liberties with her merely because she was handsome."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_GILGAMESH: "I will set up my name in the place where the names of famous men are written, and where no man's name is written I will raise a monument to the gods."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_GIRART_DE_VIENN: "May God forbid the deed![NEWLINE]A King of France shall not be harmed by me![NEWLINE]If he forgives me, I'll be his man of liege,[NEWLINE]And hold from him all my lands in fief."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_HAMMURABI: "196. If a man put out the eye of another man, his eye shall be put out."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_HONG_LOU_MENG: "The cunning waste their pains;[NEWLINE]The wise men vex their brains;[NEWLINE]But the simpleton, who seeks no gains,[NEWLINE]With belly full, he wanders free,[NEWLINE]As drifting boat upon the sea."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_IL_CANZONIERE: "How do you know, poor fool? Perhaps out there, somewhere, someone is sighing for your absence; and with this thought, my soul begins to breathe."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_JIN_PING_MEI: "The passions evoked by clouds and rain,[NEWLINE]joining their heads and twining their necks,[NEWLINE]within the bed curtains. Truly:[NEWLINE]When feelings converge, one is apt to forget[NEWLINE]what happens to a brocade girdle."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_JUSHICHIJO_KEMP: "Harmony is to be valued, and the avoidance of wanton opposition to be honored...Then what is there which cannot be accomplished."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_KEDUKAN_BUKRIT: "In the year 605 of the Saka calendar, on the eleventh day on the full moon of Waisaka, His Majesty took a boat to make a profit."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_KELLS: "In the beginning was the Word...and the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_KOKIN_WAKASHU: "Argent pearls[NEWLINE]Did my tears seem[NEWLINE]Yet with the passing years[NEWLINE]Chinese crimson[NEWLINE]Have they become"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LA_VIE_DE_GARGA: "Seeing how sorrow eats you, defeats you,[NEWLINE]I'd rather write about laughing than crying,[NEWLINE]For laughter makes men human, and courageous."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LADY_CHATTERLEY: "A woman has to live her life, or live to repent not having lived it."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LE_MORTE_DARTHU: "Yet some men say in many parts of England that King Arthur is not dead, but had by the will of our Lord Jesus into another place; and men say that he shall come again."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LEAVES_OF_GRASS: "Oh Captain! My Captain! Our fearful trip is done,[NEWLINE] The ship has weather'd every rack; the prize we sought is won."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LES_MISERABLES: "The greatest happiness in life is the conviction that we are loved - loved for ourselves, or rather, loved in spite of ourselves ... To love another person is to see the face of God."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LES_TROIS_MOUSQ: "All for one, one for all, that is our device."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LINEAR_B: "Thus saw Phugebris when the king appointed Augewas the do-mo-ko-ro...slaves women thirty-two...older girls five...younger girls fifteen...younger boys four"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_LYSISTRATA: "You, man, are fool enough, it seems, to dare to war with women, when for your faithful ally you might win us easily."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MACBETH: "Life ... is a tale[NEWLINE]Told by an idiot, full of sound and fury,[NEWLINE]Signifying nothing."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MAHABHARATA: "To save the family, abandon a man;[NEWLINE]To save the village, abandon a family;[NEWLINE]To save the country, abandon a village;[NEWLINE]To save the soul, abandon the earth."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MARIA: "I fell asleep sorrowful, filled with a vague foreboding of coming trouble. That lock of hair taken from a boy's head, that precaution of love against death, even in the presence of abounding life, caused my thoughts to wander all night about those scenes where I had passed, without knowing it, the happiest hours of my life."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MARTIN_FIERRO: "I am a gaucho, and take this from me[NEWLINE]As my tongue explains it to you:[NEWLINE]For me the earth is a small place[NEWLINE]And could be bigger yet"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MASNAVI: "Knock, and He'll open the door.[NEWLINE]Vanish, and He'll make you shine like the sun.[NEWLINE]Fall, and He'll raise you to the heavens.[NEWLINE]Become nothing, and He'll turn you into everything."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MAWANGDUI: "Bloody Hemorrhoid. Boil thoroughly one male rat in urine. Hot press with the vapor."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_METAMORPHOSES: "Happy is the man who has broken the chains which hurt the mind,[NEWLINE]and has given up worrying once and for all.[NEWLINE]Be patient and tough; one day this pain will be useful to you."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_MOBY_DICK: "Call me Ishmael. Some years ago - never mind how long precisely - having little or no money in my purse, and nothing particular to interest me on shore, I thought I would sail about a little and see the watery part of the world."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_NA_DNE: "Happiness always looks small while you hold it in your hands, but let it go, and you learn at once how big and precious it is."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_NAG_HAMMADI: "But those whom he has anointed are the ones who have become perfect. For full jars are the ones that are anointed. But when the anointing of one is dissolved, it is emptied."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ODYSSEY: "Men are so quick to blame the gods: they say[NEWLINE]that we devise their misery. But they[NEWLINE]themselves in their depravity design[NEWLINE]grief greater than the grief that fate assigns."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_OEDIPUS: "Oedipus knew the famous riddles. He was a mighty king; he was the envy of everyone. Now he's struck a wave of terrible ruin. While you are alive, you must keep looking to your final day, and do not be happy 'til you pass life's boundary without suffering grief."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ORLANDO_FURIOSO: "There never was such beauty in another man. Nature made him, and then broke the mold."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_PAPYRUS_ANI: "Hail, oh ye who give cakes and ales to perfect souls in the House of Osiris, give ye cakes and ale twice each day to the soul of Osiris Ani, whose word is true before the gods, the Lords of Abydos."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_POEMS: "Because I could not stop for Death -[NEWLINE]He kindly stopped for me -[NEWLINE]The carriage held but just Ourselves -[NEWLINE]And Immortality."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_PRESTIPLENIYE: "Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart. The really great men must, I think, have great sadness on earth."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_PRIDE_AND_PREJU: "I cannot fix on the hour, or the spot, or the look or the words, which laid the foundation. I was in the middle before I knew that I had begun."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_RAMAYAMA: "Valmiki the Poet held all the moving world inside a water drop in his hand.[NEWLINE]The gods and saints from Heaven looked down,[NEWLINE]And Valmiki looked down at the gods in the morning of Time."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_RED_BADGE_OF_CO: "He vaguely desired to walk around and around the body and stare; the impulse of the living to try to read in dead eyes the answer to the Question."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_REVIZOR: "It is no use to blame the looking glass if your face is awry."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_ROSETTA: "...those who dwell in them, and unto each and every one who hold any dignity in them, now behold, he is like unto a god..."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_RUBAIYAT: "The Moving Finger writes; and, having writ,[NEWLINE]Moves on; nor all thy piety nor wit[NEWLINE]Shall lure it back to cancel half a line,[NEWLINE]Nor all thy tears wash out a word of it."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_RUNESTONE: "I say this the second, who nine generations ago lost his life with the Hreidgoths, and died with them for his guilt."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_SAUL: "They shall record of thee, that David here[NEWLINE]Himself surrendered to ferocious Saul -[NEWLINE]March forth, O Israel, from thy peaceful tents;[NEWLINE]March forth from them, O King."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_SHINJUTEN_NO_AM: "To the ears of one possessed by the God of Death, reason and objections seem like so many idle complaints."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_SHUIHU_ZHUAN: "Today our younger brother, Chen Da, went against our advice. He offended Your Excellency and has been locked within your honorable manor. Since we have no way to save him, we have come to die with him."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_FAERIE_QUEE: "For there is nothing lost, that may be found, if sought."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_GREAT_GATSB: "Gatsby believed in the green light, the orgastic future that year by year recedes before us. It eluded us then, but that's no matter - to-morrow we will run faster, stretch out our arms farther, and one fine morning..."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_LAST_OF_THE: "Chingachgook grasped the hand that, in the warmth of feeling, the scout had stretched across the fresh earth, and in that attitude of friendship these intrepid woodsmen bowed their heads together, while scalding tears fell to their feet, watering the grave of Uncas like drops of falling rain."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_RAVEN: "Open here I flung the shutter, when, with many a flirt and flutter,[NEWLINE]In there stepped a stately raven of the saintly days of yore.[NEWLINE]Not the least obeisance made he; not a moment stopped or stayed he;[NEWLINE]But, with mien of lord or lady, perched above my chamber door."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_SIGN_OF_THE: "Once you eliminate the impossible, whatever remains, no matter how improbable, must be the truth."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_SKETCH_BOOK: "And he would have passed a pleasant life of it, in despite of the Devil and all his works, if his path had not been crossed by a being that causes more perplexity to mortal man than ghosts, goblins, and the whole race of witches put together, and that was - a woman."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_THE_TIME_MACHIN: "I think that at that time none of us quite believed in the Time Machine. The fact is, the Time Traveler was one of those men who are too clever to be believed: you never felt that you saw all around him; you always suspected some subtle reserve, some ingenuity in ambush, behind his lucid frankness."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_TRUE_STORY: "I fall back on falsehood - but falsehood of a most consistent variety; for I now make the only true statement you are to expect - that I am a liar. This defense is, I consider, a full defense against all imputations."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_TSUREZUREGUSA: "The most precious thing in life is its uncertainty. Leaving something incomplete makes it interesting and gives one the feeling that there is room for growth."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_TWICE_TOLD_TALE: "Amid the seeming confusion of our mysterious world, individuals are so nicely adjusted to a system, and systems to one another and to a whole, that, by stepping aside for a moment, a man exposes himself to a fearful risk of losing his place forever."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_UGETSU_MONOGATA: "The moon glows on the river;[NEWLINE]Wind rustles the pine.[NEWLINE]Long night, clear evening -[NEWLINE]What are they for?"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_UNCLE_TOMS_CABI: "Tom opened his eyes, and looked upon his master. 'Ye poor miserable critter!' he said, 'there ain't no more ye can do. I forgive ye, with all my soul!' and he fainted entirely away."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_URUBHANGA: "The disease of suspicion is never in my being.[NEWLINE]For I am a king who can respect the friendship[NEWLINE]That flowers between a man and a woman."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_VIGNT_MILLE_LIE: "Captain Nemo pointed to this prodigious heap of shellfish, and I saw that these mines were genuinely inexhaustible, since Nature's creative powers are greater than Man's destructive instincts."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_VOYNA_I_MIR: "You can love a person dear to you with a human love, but an enemy can only be loved with divine love."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_WAGAHAI_WA_NEKO: "Living as I do with human beings, the more that I observe them, the more I am forced to conclude that they are selfish."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_WALDEN: "I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life, and see if I could not learn what it had to teach, and not, when I came to die, discover that I had not lived."
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_QUOTE_WONDERFUL_WIZAR: "'Oh - you're a very bad man!'[NEWLINE]'Oh no, my dear. I'm a very good man. I'm just a very bad Wizard.'"
TXT_KEY_GREAT_WORK_RUBENS_AND_ISABELLA: Rubens and Isabella Brant in the Honeysuckle Bower

TXT_KEY_HANDICAP_DEITY_HELP: Only the best players in the world will beat this level.
TXT_KEY_HANDICAP_EMPEROR_HELP: Very Hard. The CPU players receive advantages to improve their play.
TXT_KEY_HANDICAP_IMMORTAL_HELP: Extremely Difficult. The CPU players receive large advantages to improve their play.
TXT_KEY_HANDICAP_KING_HELP: Hard. The CPU players receive small advantages to improve their play.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_CAUSES_HEADING2_BODY: The following increase your population's happiness:[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Natural Wonders: Each Natural Wonder you discover permanently increases your civilization's happiness.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Luxury Resources: Improve resources within your territory or trade for them with other civs. Each kind of resource improves your population's happiness (but you don't get extra happiness for having multiple copies of a single luxury).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Buildings: Certain buildings increase your population's happiness. These include the Colosseum, the Circus, the Theatre, and others. Each building constructed anywhere in your civ increases your overall happiness (so two Colosseums produce twice as much happiness as one, unlike Luxuries).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Wonders: Certain wonders like Notre Dame and the Hanging Gardens can give you a big boost in happiness.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Social Policies: Policies from the Piety branch provide a lot of happiness, as do a few policies in other branches.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Technologies: Technologies in themselves don't provide happiness, but they do unlock the buildings, wonders, resources and social policies which do.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_CAUSESUNHAPPINESS_HEAD: The following cause unhappiness:[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Raw Population: As your civ grows, the people get increasingly unhappy and demand more stuff to keep them amused.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Number of Cities: As the number of cities in your civ grows, so does your unhappiness. In other words, a civ with 2 cities each of population 1 is unhappier than a civ with 1 city of population 2, even though they both contain the same total population.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Annexed Cities: If you capture and annex foreign cities, your population doesn't much like it.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_HEADING1_BODY: Happiness is a measure of your citizens' contentment. As a rule, the larger your total population, the unhappier everybody gets. An unhappy population doesn't grow very rapidly, and a very unhappy population will affect the fighting quality of your armies as well.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Your civ's happiness is displayed on the status line of the Main Screen (in the upper left-hand corner of the game). Watch it carefully. If it reaches zero, your population is getting restless. If it starts to dip into negative numbers, you're in trouble. (Incidentally, you can get an excellent snapshot of your population's happiness by hovering your cursor over this number.)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]One important note to remember - happiness generated from city buildings and city-based policies can never exceed that city's population. For example, if a city is generating 5 happiness from buildings and a garrison, but only has 3 population, then only 3 happiness will be added to your total.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_LEVELSUNHAPPINESS_ADV_: How many levels of unhappiness can there be?
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_LEVELSUNHAPPINESS_HEAD: There are two levels of unhappiness. Unhappy, and Very Unhappy. Neither is very pleasant. An unhappy civilization can cause population growth to slow signifigantly, while a Very Unhappy civilization can have many more problems, including combat penalties, and open rebellion.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_REVOLT_HEADING3_BODY: When your unhappiness reaches -20, your population will be in "Revolt." At this stage, your empire will experience all the penalties from "Unrest", and your cities may abandon your empire, "flipping" to the civilization that is most culturally influential over your people.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_STARTING_ADV_QUEST: How much happiness does my civilization start with?
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_STARTING_HEADING2_BODY: The amount of happiness that your civilization begins with is determined by the game's difficulty setting. The moment you construct your first city, that number will begin to decline.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_UNHAPPY_HEADING3_BODY: When your happiness is negative and your happiness icon is looking sad, your population is "unhappy." An unhappy population's growth rate is significantly slowed, but there are no other ill effects.
TXT_KEY_HAPPINESS_VERYUNHAPPY_HEADING3_B: When your happiness is negative and your happiness icon is looking angry, your population is "very unhappy." If your population is very unhappy, your cities stop growing altogether, you cannot build any Settlers, and your military units get a nasty combat penalty.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Remember that unhappiness is not permanent. You can always increase your citizens' happiness - no matter how pissed off they are at you - through the methods outlined above.
TXT_KEY_HERMITAGE_THEMING_BONUS_HELP: To maximize your bonus, make sure the Great Work Slots are all filled with Art from different Eras and different Civilizations.
TXT_KEY_HURRY_GOLD: Can Spend Gold to Finish Production in a City
TXT_KEY_HURRY_POPULATION: Can Sacrifice Population to Finish Production in a City
TXT_KEY_IMPROVEMENT_BARBARIAN_CAMP_PEDIA: An Encampment is a temporary settlement by a group of rampaging barbarians intent upon overthrowing and destroying your civilization. These perpetual thorns in the sides of civilizations are capable of spitting out an unending stream of angry barbarians, all who seek to overrun your cities, pillage your lands and stare lewdly at your women. Encampments near to your cities should be destroyed without question. Encampments nearer to competing civilizations may be left to harass your foes, if you're that kind of sneaky leader...
TXT_KEY_IMPROVEMENT_CITY_RUINS_PEDIA: City Ruins are the remnants of cities destroyed in the game. They are of no particular value in themselves, but they often sit atop valuable locations. A worker may clean up a City Ruin or simply replace it with another improvement.
TXT_KEY_IMPROVEMENT_GOODY_HUT_PEDIA: Ancient Ruins are the remains of earlier long-dead civilizations. They are filled with valuable secrets - gold, maps, lost technologies and sometimes even survivors - awaiting discovery by the intrepid explorer. Rich rewards await those who first enter an Ancient Ruin!

TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_PROJECT_WORLD_FAIR_SPLASH: Thinkers and inventors from around the world have come together to share their ideas in the extravagant World's Fair.
TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_PROJECT_WORLD_GAMES_SPLAS: Athletes representing Civilizations from around the world have gathered to compete in feats of strength, skill, and grace. As the world watches, Civilizations rise to glory with each medal earned in the International Games.
TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_SPECIAL_SESSION_LEADERSHI: The voices of nations large and small fight to be heard and international affairs become more complex. A special session is called to choose the next host to lead the Congress forward.
TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_SPECIAL_SESSION_START_UNI: The fledgling idea of a permanent peace takes root in the World Congress, which is reborn as the United Nations. A special session to is called to choose the host of this powerful new diplomatic force. The United Nations will continue to decide matters as before, but regular sessions will alternate with sessions attempting to choose a World Leader, making [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Diplomatic Victory[ENDCOLOR] possible.
TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_SPECIAL_SESSION_START_WOR: Leaders from around the world come together in the World Congress to begin discussing international affairs. Their first order of business is to determine who will host the Congress.
TXT_KEY_LEAGUE_SPECIAL_SESSION_WELCOME_C: City-States find their place on the world stage and formally enter international diplomacy. Civilizations now receive additional delegates from having City-State allies. Amidst this change, the World Congress calls a special session to determine the next host of the Congress.

TXT_KEY_MEDIEVAL_SCENARIO_RELIGION_CHRIS: Roman Catholicism refers to the theology and doctrine of the Christian church under the auspices of the Holy See, the papacy based in Rome. According to ecclesiastical scholars, Catholicism is distinguished from other branches of Christianity by its commitment to the church's traditions, to the sacraments, and its priesthood as interpreters of the Bible.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]The earliest known use of the term "Catholic Church" is by Ignatius of Antioch around 107 AD. The early church was organized based on the rule of three patriarchs, those of Rome, Alexandria and Antioch - to which were later added the patriarchs of Constantinople and Jerusalem. The patriarch of Rome claimed special authority based on the connection between St. Peter and the city. For 1000 years, Roman Catholicism dominated Europe, until the Great Schism of 1054 AD that gave rise to Eastern Orthodoxy. Some 500 years later another major division in Christianity occurred with the Protestant Reformation, which saw many in Europe reject the teachings and practices of Catholicism in favor of more personal expressions of belief.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Presently, the Roman Catholic Church considers those not in communion with the Vatican to be "non-Catholics" but tolerates differing interpretations of the Bible. Roman Catholicism remains the largest Christian church, with over a billion members worldwide.
TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_AT_WAR: We're at war! Build military units and buildings to achieve victory!
TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_EMPIRE_DEFENS: We need more military units to help defend our civilization.

TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_ENOUGH_MILITA: We're spending a lot on military maintenance. We should stop building more military units.
TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_ERADICATE_BAR: It's time for our barbarian neighbors to go extinct. Build military units and use them to destroy the camps.
TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_LOSING_WARS: We're losing! We should consider negotiating a peace treaty.
TXT_KEY_MILITARYAISTRATEGY_WINNING_WARS: We're winning! Keep the pressure on and claim victory!
TXT_KEY_MISSION_AIRLIFT_HELP: Airlifts the unit to another city.

TXT_KEY_MISSION_BUY_CITY_STATE_HELP: The Merchant of Venice will use his considerable wealth to purchase a City State and put it under control of Venice as a puppeted city. You gain control of all of the minor civilization's existing units.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CHANGE_ADMIRAL_PORT_HELP: Move this Admiral to another port city under your control. It can be moved out of the other port next turn.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CHANGE_TRADE_UNIT_HOME_C: Change the home city of this trade unit. It will arrive at the other city next turn. This allows it to make trade routes from its new home city.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CONDUCT_TRADE_MISSION_DI: Unit must be in a City State's territory that you're not at war with.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CONDUCT_TRADE_MISSION_HE: If the unit is inside a City State's territory that you're not at war with, this order will have the Great Merchant conduct a trade mission, giving you a large amount of [ICON_GOLD] Gold and improving relations with the City State. It consumes the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CONSTRUCT_HELP: This will have the Great Person create their special building. It consumes the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CREATE_GREAT_WORK_DISABL: Unit must be in or adjacent to one of your cities and there must be a Great Work slot of the appropriate type available somewhere in your empire.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CREATE_GREAT_WORK_HELP: This order will have the Great Artist create a Great Work in the city they are currently in. It consumes the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CULTURE_BOMB_DISABLED_HE: Unit must be in or next to a tile owned by you, and may not be in another player's territory.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_CULTURE_BOMB_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and cause all tiles this unit is on and adjacent to become part of your borders, even if they belong to another player. This will have diplomatic repercussions!
TXT_KEY_MISSION_DISCOVER_TECH_DISABLED_H: You must control a city before you can discover new technology.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_DISCOVER_TECH_HELP: This will have the Great Person help research a technology. It consumes the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_DISEMBARK_HELP: Unloads Unit from boats back onto a land tile.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_EMBARK_HELP: Loads Unit onto boats, allowing them to cross water tiles.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ENHANCE_RELIGION_DISABLE: Unit must be in the Holy City for this religion.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ENHANCE_RELIGION_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and will allow you to add two new beliefs to your religion. You will also have the option to buy Inquisitors to defend your religion.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ESTABLISH_TRADE_ROUTE_HE: Establish a Trade Route with this unit from this city to another city.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_FORTIFY_HELP: The Unit remains inactive until the player provides it new orders. The unit receives a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_FOUND_RELIGION_DISABLED_: Unit must be in one of your cities.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_FOUND_RELIGION_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and will result in the creation of a new religion.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_GARRISON_DISABLED_HELP: Unit cannot Garrison if already garrisoned or if another Unit is garrisoned in this City.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_GARRISON_HELP: Garrison a unit in this city, improving its [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_GIVE_POLICIES_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and will grant a large amount of [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_HEAL_DISABLED_HELP: This Unit cannot Heal outside friendly territory.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_HEAL_HELP: The unit remains inactive until it heals back to full strength. The unit receives a defensive bonus.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_HURRY_PRODUCTION_HELP: This order will hurry production on the city's current effort. It consumes the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_INTERCEPT_HELP: This air unit order will tell the craft to attempt interception with any incoming air attacks.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_JOIN_CITY_HELP: This order will make the Great Person join the city as a super specialist. It will consume the Great Person.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_MOVE_TO_HELP: Order your unit to move to a specific Tile.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_MOVE_TO_UNIT_HELP: Set your unit to move to another unit.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_NUKE_HELP: Launches a nuclear weapon upon the chosen Tile.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ONE_SHOT_TOURISM_DISABLE: Unit must be in a tile owned by another major Civilization.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ONE_SHOT_TOURISM_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and will spread a large quantity of [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism to the civilization where the Musician is located. Other civs in the game also receive 20% of this [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_PARADROP_HELP: Paradrops to a specified location. The mission is subject to interception.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_PILLAGE_HELP: An improvement or route in this tile may not be used until repaired. Pillaging an improvement will earn [ICON_GOLD] Gold and heal your unit by 25 points.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_PLUNDER_TRADE_ROUTE_DISA: You must be able to declare war against the trade route owner.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_PLUNDER_TRADE_ROUTE_HELP: Destroy this trade route to gain [ICON_GOLD] Gold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]If you are not at war with the owner of the trade route, you will need to declare war before you plunder. Plundering a trade route going to another civilization will damage your relationship with the destination civilization if you're not currently at war.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_REMOVE_HERESY_DISABLED_H: Unit must be in or adjacent to a friendly city.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_REMOVE_HERESY_HELP: This order will remove the presence of other religions from the nearby, friendly-controlled city. An inquisitor is consumed by this action.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_REPAIR_FLEET_HELP: This order will consume the Great Admiral and heal all of your naval units and embarked units in this tile and all adjacent tiles.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_ROUTE_TO_HELP: Tells a Worker to construct a Road (or Railroad) to the chosen location. The Worker will follow the shortest path to the location. The Worker wakes when it has completed the assignment.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SELL_EXOTIC_GOODS_DISABL: Unit must be next to a tile owned by another Civilization or City-State, and must not have already used this ability.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SELL_EXOTIC_GOODS_HELP: This unit will sell its cargo, earning [ICON_GOLD] Gold and XP. More will be earned for selling farther from your capital. This may only be done once with this unit.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SET_UP_HELP: Required before this Unit can Range Attack.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SKIP_HELP: The Unit will perform no action this turn.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SLEEP_HELP: Order the Unit to remain inactive until the player provides it new orders.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SPREAD_RELIGION_DISABLED: Unit must be in or adjacent to a city (it does not have to be a friendly city).
TXT_KEY_MISSION_SPREAD_RELIGION_HELP: This order will convert some of the citizens of the nearby city to the unit's religion. This unit will be consumed if this is their final Spread Religion action. Spreading by Great Prophets also removes the influence of other religions.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_START_GOLDENAGE_DISABLED: Unit must be in your territory to start a Golden Age.
TXT_KEY_MISSION_START_GOLDENAGE_HELP: This order will consume the Great Person and initiate a [ICON_GOLDEN_AGE] Golden Age (extra [ICON_PRODUCTION] and [ICON_GOLD]) for {1_Num} turns. Each time a Great Person starts a Golden Age, the length of the next will shorten (to a minimum of 3 turns).
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_ATTACKORDERS_ADV_QUEST: How does movement work with attack orders?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_ATTACKORDERS_HEADING3_B: Generally, if you order a unit to move into a space occupied by an enemy unit, the unit will interpret that order as instructions to attack the enemy unit. If the moving unit is non-combat, the unit will stop and ask for new orders.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_COMBAT_ADV_QUEST: How does movement work in relation to combat?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_COMBAT_HEADING2_BODY: Movement rules are modified when enemy forces are involved.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_CONTROLZONES_ADV_QUEST: How do zones of control effect movement?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_CONTROLZONES_HEADING3_B: Combat units exert a "Zone of Control" (ZOC) over the tiles around them. When a unit moves between two tiles within an enemy's ZOC it expends all of its MPs.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_EMBARKING_ADV_QUEST: How can land units move across water?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_EMBARKING_HEADING3_BODY: At the start of the game, your land units cannot enter any water tiles. However, once you've learned the Optics technology, you can earn the promotion that allows land units to "embark" and move into coastal water tiles. To embark a unit, move the unit to a coastal tile and then click on the "Embark" Action. Once embarked, the unit must move into water. (Optics allows movement into coastal water only. The later Astronomy tech allows embarked units to enter ocean tiles.)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]In the water the embarked unit is very slow and helpless. It is totally unable to fight, and any enemy naval vessel can easily destroy it. It's critical to accompany embarked land units with a strong naval defense.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]When the unit is adjacent to a land tile, you can click on the "Disembark" action. The unit will then be able to return to dry land. Alternatively you can right-click on a land tile and the unit will disembark automatically.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_EXPENDINGPOINTS_HEADING: Units expend MPs to enter tiles. The terrain of the tile a unit is entering determines the MP cost of the move. It doesn't cost anything to leave your current tile; the MP cost is determined by the tile you're entering.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]See the terrain rules for details on MP costs, but generally, open terrain like Grassland and Plains costs 1 MP to enter, while Forest and Jungle costs 2. It also expends all of a unit's MPs to cross a river (unless a road is there).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]A unit can always move one tile if it has any MPs left. It doesn't matter how expensive the tile is; as long as the unit has something left, it can enter. Once the unit has expended all of its MPs, it must stop moving.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_HEADING1_BODY: During a game of Civilization V, much of your time will be spent moving units around the world. You'll be marching your military units off to discover stuff or to fight with your neighbors. Your workers will be moving to new tiles to improve stuff and to construct roads. Your settlers will be moving to good locations on which to build new cities.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Following are rules for moving land units and naval units. Air units have their own special rules; since they don't occur until late in the game, they're covered elsewhere.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_HOWTOORDER_ADV_QUEST: What are the ways to move a unit?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_HOWTOORDER_HEADING2_BOD: There are two ways to move a unit: using right-click and move mode.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_HOWTOORDER_HEADING2_TIT: How to Order a Unit to Move
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_ILLEGAL_HEADING2_BODY: Certain tiles cannot be entered by certain units. A naval unit can't enter a non-city land tile, for example, and a land unit cannot enter a mountain tile or an ocean tile. If a unit can't enter a tile, you won't be able to order it to move there. Sometimes a move is revealed as illegal during a unit's move. If that is the case, the unit will stop when it discovers the illegality and wait for new orders.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_LEGAL_ADV_QUEST: What makes moves legal and illegal?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_LEGAL_HEADING3_BODY: If the location is illegal for the unit, it will decline the order and wait for new instructions.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]If the location is legal and the unit can reach that location in one turn, it will do so.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_MOVEMODE_HEADING3_BODY: You can also click on the "Move Mode" Action button, then left-click on a target space.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_MULTIPLETURN_ADV_QUEST: How does movement across multiple turns work?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_MULTIPLETURN_HEADING2_B: If the unit requires multiple turns to reach the location, it will pick the shortest route and proceed on its way. It will continue to move each turn until it gets to the assigned spot.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]If it becomes impossible for the unit to reach its assigned location - say, because exploration reveals that the tile is across the ocean and the moving unit can't embark, or perhaps because another unit has sat down in the target location - the unit will stop and request new orders.[NEWLINE]You can change a unit's orders at any time by clicking on the unit and then either giving it new orders or clicking on the "Cancel Orders" action.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_NAVAL_HEADING2_BODY: Generally, naval units follow the same rules as land units, except that they move in the water rather than on land. Early naval units are often limited to coastal waters (those adjacent to land tiles) and coastal cities. Eventually, you'll produce naval units that can enter deep ocean tiles, and thus explore the world. Naval units generally cannot enter ice tiles (except for submarines, which can go under 'em).
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_POINTS_HEADING2_BODY: All mobile units have a certain number of "Movement Points" (MPs) that they can expend on movement in every turn. Once they've expended those MPs, they can't move any more until the next turn.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Most early land units have 2 MPs; horse and naval units have more.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_RAILANDROADS_ADV_QUEST: How do roads and railroads effect movement?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_RAILANDROADS_HEADING2_B: Roads and railroads cut a unit's movement cost in friendly or neutral territory. As long as the unit moves from one tile containing a road/railroad into another tile containing a road/railroad, the unit will expend just a fraction of the normal cost to move. As long as the unit has any MPs left, it can continue to move along the road/railroad.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_RIGHTCLICK_HEADING3_BOD: When a unit is active, you can right-click anywhere on the map to order the unit to move there.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_RIVERS_ADV_QUEST: How do roads interact with rivers?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_RIVERS_HEADING3_BODY: Once you've learned the "Engineering" tech, you can move across rivers on roads/railroads without paying the standard penalty for crossing the river. If you don't have Engineering yet, you must pay the penalty even if crossing over on a road.
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_STACKINGLIMITS_ADV_QUES: What are the stacking limitations in a tile?
TXT_KEY_MOVEMENT_STACKINGLIMITS_HEADING3: Remember that only one combat unit can end its turn in a tile, and only one non-combat unit can end its move in a tile - though a single combat unit and a single non-combat unit can end their turn "stacked" in the same tile.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]A unit may pass through another unit as long as it has enough movement to complete the full move, and does not end up on top of another unit of the same type.
TXT_KEY_MP_OPTION_ALWAYS_PEACE_HELP: Players are not allowed to go to war with one another.
TXT_KEY_MP_OPTION_TAKEOVER_AI_HELP: Players can "hot-join" into a CPU civilization's slot at any time
TXT_KEY_MP_OPTION_TURN_TIMER_HELP: Turns are limited by a timer which scales based on the size of the largest civilization
TXT_KEY_MUSEUM_THEMING_BONUS_HELP: To maximize your bonus, make sure both Great Works are of the same type (both Art or both Artifact), both from the same Era, and either both from the city's civilization, or both from civilizations other than the city owner.
TXT_KEY_NEWWORLD_SCENARIO_CIV5_RESOURCE_: The North American bison, which once covered the Great Plains in massive herds, was the primary source for the meat, sinew, hide and bone necessary for survival by many native tribes. Hunted to near extinction by Americans in the late 1800s, in recent decades the buffalo has made a comeback due to federal and state wildlife programs.

Cocoa beans, the product of the cacao tree native to South America, was an important commodity in pre-Columbian Mesoamerica, and figured prominently in tribal cultures. Introduced by the Spanish to Europe, by the mid-1700s it was immensely popular there as a beverage, cooking ingredient, and even as a medicine.
TXT_KEY_NO_ACTION_SETTLER_SIZE_LIMIT_HAR: [NEWLINE]Settlers may only be built in Cities with at least 2 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens.
TXT_KEY_OXFORD_UNIVERSITY_THEMING_BONUS_: To maximize your bonus, make sure the Great Work Slots are all filled with Writings from different Eras and from Civilizations other than the city owner.
TXT_KEY_PEDIA_PROMOTION_MUST_SET_UP: Must Set Up to Ranged Attack
TXT_KEY_PEDIA_PROMOTION_PARTIAL_HEAL_IF_: Heals 2 Damage If Kills a Unit
TXT_KEY_PLAYER_OPTION_NO_UNIT_CYCLING_HE: Do Not Automatically Select The Next Unit
TXT_KEY_PLAYER_OPTION_QUICK_MOVES_HELP: All of the player's moves are accelerated
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ACADEMY_SCIENCES_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Academy of Sciences[ENDCOLOR][NEWLINE]+1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Local Happiness from every Observatory, Public School, and Research Lab.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ACADEMY_SCIENCES_TEXT: An Academy of Sciences is a state-supported institution of learning or research society dedicated to the sciences and/or technology. The most famous, the Russian - later Soviet - Academy was founded in 1724 AD by Peter the Great in his efforts to modernize his empire. In 1917 the academy came under Soviet control, and was tasked with addressing questions of construction and social issues; beginning in 1928 the Soviet Politburo increasingly interfered in the teaching and research of the Soviet Academy of Sciences, hindering the scientific progress of the Soviet Union for decades.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_AESTHETICS_TEXT: Aesthetics is the policy which is concerned with the creation and appreciation of beauty. The search for beauty (and its sister, truth) is the highest of all human endeavors, the heart and soul of life itself. Many cultures throughout history have created and appreciated beautiful things. The statues and architecture of ancient Greece, the plays and poetry of England, the French Impressionists: all of these and more have brought joy and astonishment to countless millions of viewers.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ARISTOCRACY_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Aristocracy[ENDCOLOR][NEWLINE]+15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Wonders and +1 [ICON_HAPPINESS_1] Happiness for every 10 [ICON_CITIZEN] Citizens in a City.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ARISTOCRACY_TEXT: Aristocracy is a form of government in which a group of the most prominent citizens rule. Membership to this group may be hereditary, or it may be through merit or wealth or all three. The term is derived from the Greek word aristokratia, meaning "the rule of the best." There is a very thin line between an aristocracy and an oligarchy, and which form a government actually is might very well depend upon one's point of view: the rulers might consider themselves aristocrats, while everybody else sees them as oligarchs.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ARSENAL_DEMOCRACY_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Arsenal of Democracy[ENDCOLOR][NEWLINE]+15% [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production when building Military Units. +15 [ICON_INFLUENCE] Influence from Military Unit gifts to City-States.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ARSENAL_DEMOCRACY_TEXT: "Arsenal of Democracy" was a phrase coined by President Franklin Roosevelt during a radio broadcast in December 1940 to describe America's role in the struggle against totalitarianism. The unofficial policy of the United States during World War II and the ensuing Cold War against Communism, the "Arsenal" refers to committing a nation's private industry to producing military materials for the nation and other nations with the goal of defeating a common enemy. The privately-owned manufacturers are compensated at market value, usually on a "cash-and-carry" basis.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_ARTISTIC_GENIUS_TEXT: An artistic genius might be defined as someone who has the creativity, originality and skill to bring about beauty in an art form, whatever it may be. Artistic genius can even be applied to performance, as with choreography, orchestration or theater. The history of civilization is dotted with literary, artistic and performance geniuses, often temperamental but always brilliant in their field.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_AESTHETICS_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Aesthetics[ENDCOLOR] improves the civilization's ability to generate [ICON_CULTURE] Culture.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting Aesthetics allows you to earn Great Writers, Artists, and Musicians 25% faster. Unlocks building the Uffizi.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all policies doubles the theming bonus you receive from Museums and Wonders. It also allows the purchase of Great Writers, Artists, or Musicians with [ICON_PEACE] Faith starting in the Industrial Era.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_AUTOCRACY_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Autocracy[ENDCOLOR] is for militaristic civilizations, dreaming of world conquest.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting Autocracy reduces Unit [ICON_GOLD] Gold Maintenance costs by 33%, allowing an empire to field a larger military.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all Policies in the Autocracy tree will grant a 20% attack bonus to all Military Units for 30 turns.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][COLOR_WARNING_TEXT]This branch cannot be active at the same time as Freedom or Order.[ENDCOLOR]
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_COMMERCE_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Commerce[ENDCOLOR] provides bonuses to naval empires, and those focused on [ICON_GOLD] Gold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting Commerce will boost [ICON_GOLD] Gold output in [ICON_CAPITAL] Capital City by 25%.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all Policies in the Commerce tree will grant +1 [ICON_GOLD] Gold from every Specialist.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_EXPLORATION_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Exploration[ENDCOLOR] enhances your abilties to spread your empire over the seas.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting Exploration gives +1 [ICON_MOVES] Movement for Naval units and +1 Sight for Naval combat units. Unlocks building the Louvre.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all policies allows you to see Hidden Antiquity Sites. It also allows the purchase of Great Admirals with [ICON_PEACE] Faith starting in the Industrial Era.
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_FREEDOM_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Freedom[ENDCOLOR] is best for small, focused empires. In particular, it provides bonuses for [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People and Specialists.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Upon adopting Freedom, the rate at which [ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE] Great People are born is increased by 25%.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all Policies in the Freedom tree will increase the base yield from Great Tile Improvements by 100% and length of [ICON_GOLDEN_AGE] Golden Ages increased by 50%.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][COLOR_WARNING_TEXT]This branch cannot be active at the same time as Autocracy or Order.[ENDCOLOR]
TXT_KEY_POLICY_BRANCH_HONOR_HELP: [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Honor[ENDCOLOR] improves the effectiveness of one's army in a variety of ways.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting Honor gives a +25% combat bonus VS Barbarians, and notifications will be provided when new Barbarian Encampments spawn in revealed territory. Gain [ICON_CULTURE] Culture for the empire from each barbarian killed.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Adopting all policies in the Honor tree will grant [ICON_GOLD] Gold for each enemy unit killed.
