TXT_KEY_UNIT_ASSYRIAN_SIEGE_TOWER_STRATE: This melee siege unit must move next to an enemy city to be effective, but once it has, it is devastating. It not only has a powerful city attack of its own, but also confers a bonus to any of your nearby units also attacking the city. Complement a Siege Tower with melee or ranged units, and make sure it reaches its target before getting killed, and cities will fall much more quickly.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_AT_GUN_STRATEGY: The Anti-Tank Gun is a specialized combat unit which is quite potent against enemy tanks, but weak when battling other units. Back them up with tanks of your own and artillery for a potent fighting force.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ATOMIC_BOMB_STRATEGY: An Atomic Bomb is an extremely powerful unit. The Atomic Bomb can be based in a player-owned city or aboard a carrier. It can move from base to base or attack a target within its range of 10 tiles. When it detonates, an Atomic Bomb will damage or possibly destroy units, and cities will be severely damaged, within its blast radius of 2 tiles. It is automatically destroyed when it attacks. See the rules on Nuclear Weapons for more details.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_AUSTRIAN_HUSSAR_STRATEGY: Hussars are great scouts and flank attackers. They are slightly faster than Cavalry which they replace. They start with Promotions that give them +1 Sight and increases their bonus when making a Flank Attack by 50%.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_AXMAN_STRATEGY: Stronger than an archer, but limited by its one-hex range.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_AZTEC_JAGUAR_STRATEGY: The Jaguar is the Aztec unique unit, replacing the Warrior. The Jaguar receives a big bonus when battling in Jungle. It also heals damage when it destroys enemy units and has the Woodsman promotion, allowing it to move through Forest and Jungle quickly.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BATTLESHIP_STRATEGY: The Battleship is an extremely powerful warship with a mighty Combat Strength and Ranged Combat Strength. It has an attack range of 3 hexes, allowing it to bombard units or cities several hexes inland. Its indirect fire ability allows it to bombard targets it cannot see (as long as other friendly units can see them). The Battleship is vulnerable to air and submarine attacks.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BAZOOKA_STRATEGY: The Bazooka is the last non-siege ranged unit. It is the most powerful ranged unit. Despite having only a one-hex range it can still deal significant damage to other units without being hurt itself. This makes it a great defense unit.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BERBER_CAVALRY_STRATEGY: This mounted unit replaces Cavalry and is a fierce warrior in the deserts of Morocco. Receives combat bonuses for both fighting in a Desert tile or fighting in Moroccan territory (these bonuses do stack).
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BOMBER_STRATEGY: The Bomber is an air unit. It is effective against ground targets, less so against naval targets, and it's quite vulnerable to enemy aircraft. The Bomber can be based on a player-owned city or aboard a carrier. It can move from base to base and perform missions within its range of "10." Use Bombers to attack enemy units and cities. When possible, send in fighters first to "use up" the enemy's anti-aircraft defenses for that turn. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BRAZILIAN_PRACINHA_STRATEGY: This melee unit earns points toward starting the next Carnival for Brazil when it defeats an enemy. Use them to fight wars in foreign lands to push Brazil toward a Culture victory.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BYZANTINE_CATAPHRACT_STRATE: The Cataphract is a Byzantine unique unit, replacing the Horseman. Cataphracts are slower but more powerful than the Horseman unit, making them one of the most dangerous cavalry units until the arrival of the Knight. They can move after attacking. They are able to gain defensive bonuses, unlike the Horseman. Also their penalty for attacking cities is not as severe.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_BYZANTINE_DROMON_STRATEGY: Only the Byzantines may build this unit. Attacks with lethal Greek Fire, making it the first naval unit with a ranged attack. This unit fights at +50% strength against other naval units. It cannot enter Deep Ocean tiles outside of the city borders.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CANNON_STRATEGY: The Cannon is an important mid-game siege unit, devastating against civilizations which have not yet acquired them. Like other siege units, the Cannon must spend 1 mp to set up before it can attack. Cannon remain potent into the Industrial era. Cannon cannot see as far as other units, and should be protected with melee units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARAVAN_STRATEGY: A Caravan allow you to establish a land-based Trade Route between two civilizations, earning [ICON_GOLD] Gold for both the owner and the destination civilization. Building a Granary or a Workshop in its home city also allows you to shuttle [ICON_FOOD] Food or [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production among your own cities. Once you assign it to a route, it cannot defend itself, so make sure the route is clear of danger.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARAVEL_STRATEGY: The Caravel is a significant upgrade to a civilization's naval power. A Ranged unit, it is stronger and faster than a Trireme, and it can enter Deep Ocean hexes. The Caravel has a huge sight radius, making it the eyes and ears of the mid-game navy. This sight bonus is lost if the Caravel is upgraded to a Destroyer.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARGO_SHIP_STRATEGY: A Cargo Ship allows you to establish a sea-based Trade Route between two civilizations, earning [ICON_GOLD] Gold for both the owner and the destination civilization. It also has a much longer range than the land-based Caravans. Building a Granary or a Workshop in its home city also allows you to shuttle [ICON_FOOD] Food or [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production among your own cities. Once you assign it to a route, it cannot defend itself, so make sure the route is clear of danger.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARRIER_STRATEGY: The Carrier is a specialized vessel which carries fighter airplanes, bombers (but not stealth bombers), and atomic bombs. The Carrier itself is unable to attack, but the aircraft it carries make it the most powerful offensive weapon afloat. Defensively, however, the Carrier is weak and should be escorted by destroyers and submarines.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARTHAGINIAN_FOREST_ELEPHAN: The African Forest Elephant is a Carthaginian unique unit, replacing the Horseman. The Carthaginian Elephants are slower but more powerful than the Horseman. They can move after attacking. They also strike fear into nearby enemy units, giving them all a negative combat modifier. Success in combat with an Elephant has an increased chance of generating Great Generals.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CARTHAGINIAN_QUINQUEREME_ST: Only the Carthaginians may build this unit. It is a stronger version of the Trireme, including in Combat Strength. Like the Trireme it is a melee attack unit, engaging units at sea or cities (but it has no ranged attack). The Quinquereme cannot enter Deep Ocean tiles outside of the city borders.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CATAPULT_STRATEGY: The Catapult is a siege weapon, extremely useful when assaulting early enemy cities. It is slow and extremely vulnerable to enemy melee attack; it always should be supported by other units when it's in the field. The Catapult must "set up" (1 Movement Point) before attacking.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CAVALRY_STRATEGY: The Cavalry mounted units are devastating against melee, ranged and siege weapons, but less effective against other mounted units. Use them to destroy Musketmen and then break through the enemy's lines with their ability to move after attacking. Cavalry are quite vulnerable to the mounted-unit-killing Lancer.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CELT_PICTISH_WARRIOR_STRATE: The Pictish Warrior is the Celt unique unit. Perfectly suited for early rushes, a Pictish Warrior can lay waste to an opponents' lands, quickly pillaging and moving within the same turn. Additionally, they earn [ICON_PEACE] Faith for each unit they kill, constantly advancing your religious goals while at war.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CHARIOT_ARCHER_STRATEGY: Chariot Archers are fast ranged units, deadly on open terrain. They are slightly more powerful than the Archer and can move twice the distance. However, Chariot Archers suffer a movement penalty when entering Forest, Jungle or Hill hexes (unless roads have been constructed in them). As a mounted unit, the Chariot Archer is vulnerable to Spearmen.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CHINESE_CHUKONU_STRATEGY: The Chu-Ko-Nu is the Chinese unique unit, replacing the Crossbowman. The Chu-Ko-Nu is slightly less powerful than the Crossbowman, but it can attack twice during a turn, giving it a hefty offensive punch. This unit is particularly deadly when garrisoned in a city that is under attack.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_COMPOSITE_BOWMAN_STRATEGY: The Composite Bowman is more powerful than the Archer. As a Ranged unit it can deal significant damage to other units up to two hexes away, but it is vulnerable to melee attack. Put Composite Bowman in your city for defense, and guard them with melee units when on the move.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_CROSSBOWMAN_STRATEGY: The Crossbowman is much more powerful than the Archer unit. As a Ranged unit it can deal significant damage to other Medieval Era units up to two hexes away, but it is vulnerable to melee attack. Put Crossbowmen in your city for defense, and guard them with melee units when on the move.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_DESTROYER_STRATEGY: The Destroyer is designed to hunt down and destroy submarines and to provide anti-aircraft support. It often operates as an escort to embarked units, battleships and carriers when in range of enemy planes and subs. The Destroyer is shockingly fast and its indirect fire ability allows it to bombard targets it cannot see (as long as other friendly units can see them).
TXT_KEY_UNIT_EGYPTIAN_WARCHARIOT_STRATEG: The War Chariot is the Egyptian unique unit, replacing the Chariot Archer. It is faster than the standard Chariot Archer, and the Egyptian player can construct it even if he doesn't possess the [ICON_RES_HORSE] Horse resource. A mounted unit, the War Chariot is quite vulnerable to the Spearman.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ENGLISH_LONGBOWMAN_STRATEGY: The Longbowman is the English unique unit, replacing the Crossbowman. The Longbowman has a greater range than the Crossbowman, allowing it to attack enemies three hexes away, often destroying them before they can strike back. Like other ranged units, Longbowmen are vulnerable to melee attack.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ENGLISH_SHIPOFTHELINE_STRAT: The Ship of the Line is the English unique unit, replacing the Frigate. The Ship of the Line has a slightly higher Ranged Combat strength than the Frigate, and it's slightly cheaper to produce. It can see one hex farther than the Frigate, allowing it to spot enemies in a significantly larger section of ocean.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_FIGHTER_STRATEGY: The Fighter is a moderately-powerful air unit. It can be based in any city you own or aboard an aircraft carrier. It can move from city to city (or carrier) and can perform "missions" within its range of 8 tiles. Use fighters to attack enemy aircraft and ground units, to scout enemy positions, and to defend against enemy air attacks. Fighters are especially effective against enemy helicopters. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_FRENCH_FOREIGNLEGION_STRATE: The Foreign Legion is a French unique unit, replacing the infantry. It receives a significant combat bonus when operating outside of home territory, making it an excellent unit to launch against enemy territory.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_FRENCH_MUSKETEER_STRATEGY: The Musketeer is the French unique unit, replacing the Musketman. The Musketeer is significantly more powerful than the Musketman, making it the most powerful foot soldier unit in the game (until the introduction of Industrial Era Infantry units).
TXT_KEY_UNIT_FRIGATE_STRATEGY: The Frigate is the first naval unit since the Caravel. It is slightly slower than the Caravel, but its Combat and Ranged Combat strengths are more than twice as large as the older naval unit. The Frigate can clear the seas of any Caravels, Triremes, and Barbarian units still afloat.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GALLEASS_STRATEGY: The Galleass is the first naval unit with a ranged attack generally available to the civilizations in the game. It is much stronger than earlier naval units but a bit slower. It cannot initiate melee combat. The Galleass is useful for clearing enemy ships out of shallow waters. It cannot enter Deep Ocean hexes unless it is from Polynesia.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GALLEY_PEDIA: The Galley is a Barbarian vessel. It is a ship propelled by human oarsmen, in every way inferior to the trireme, the first vessel accessible to civilizations. Generally, Galleys can be expected to haunt the coasts of civilizations, harassing shipping and capturing embarked units. They should be destroyed upon sight.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GALLEY_STRATEGY: The Galley is a Barbarian unit which remains in coastal hexes, looking for embarked units to destroy. It is far weaker than any other naval units, but can be dangerous in packs. A ranged unit, the Galley can annoy cities and units near the coast.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GATLINGGUN_STRATEGY: The Gatling Gun is a mid-game non-siege ranged unit. It is much more powerful than earlier ranged units like the Crossbowman. It has only a one-hex range, but still can deal significant damage to other units without being hurt itself. Put Gatling Guns in your city for defense.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GERMAN_LANDSKNECHT_STRATEGY: The Landsknecht is a German unique unit. This unit replaces the Pikeman unit. The Landsknecht is as powerful as the Pikeman unit, but it costs only half as much to produce, allowing the German player to build up a medieval army in a shockingly short period of time. Landsknecht units are deadly against mounted units like Knights.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GERMAN_PANZER_STRATEGY: The Panzer is the German unique unit, replacing the tank. It is both stronger and faster than the tank. Like the tank the Panzer gets a combat penalty when attacking cities. It also can move after combat, allowing it to blow huge holes in enemy lines and then barrel through before the enemy can repair the gap.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_ADMIRAL_STRATEGY: The Great Admiral has the ability to instantly repair every naval and embarked unit in the same hex, as well as in adjacent hexes. The Great Admiral is consumed when performing this action. The Great Admiral also provides a 15% combat bonus to all player-owned naval units within 2 tiles. The Great Admiral is NOT consumed when he provides this bonus.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_ARTIST_STRATEGY: Great Artists can construct the special Landmark improvements, which, when worked, provide loads of [ICON_CULTURE] Culture to a city. The Great Artist can also "culture bomb" a tile inside or within 1 space of your territory, putting that tile and all adjacent tiles into your territory. Great Artists can also create Golden Ages. Great Artists are expended when used in any of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_ENGINEER_STRATEGY: Great Engineers can construct the special Manufactory improvement, which, when worked, provides a lot of [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production for a city. The Great Engineer can also hurry production of a unit, building or Wonder in a city. The Great Engineer can also create a Golden Age. The Great Engineer is expended when used in any of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_GENERAL_STRATEGY: The Great General can construct the special Citadel improvement which gives a big defensive bonus to any unit occupying it. The Great General is consumed when it builds the Citadel. The Great General provides a 20% combat bonus to all player-owned units within 2 tiles. The Great General is NOT consumed when he provides this bonus. The Great General can also initiate a Golden Age.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_MERCHANT_STRATEGY: The Great Merchant can construct the special Custom House improvement which, when worked, produces loads of [ICON_GOLD] Gold. The Great Merchant can also journey to a city-state and perform a "trade mission" which produces gobs of [ICON_GOLD] Gold and garners many Influence Points with the city-state. Finally, the Great Merchant can create a Golden Age. The Great Merchant is expended when used in any of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_MUSICIAN_STRATEGY: A Great Musician can create a Great Work of Music (generates both [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism) that is placed in the nearest city that has an appropriate building with an empty slot (like an Opera House or Broadcast Tower). A Great Musician can also travel to another civilization and perform a Concert Tour, which will give you a one-time boost in [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism in that territory. Great Musicians are expended when used either of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_PROPHET_STRATEGY: The Great Prophet is the only unit who can found or enhance a religion. He can also construct the special Holy Site improvement which, when worked, produces lots of [ICON_PEACE] Faith. Alternatively, the Great Prophet can spread religion 4 times (displacing any previous religions in these cities). The Great Prophet is expended when used in any of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_SCIENTIST_STRATEGY: The Great Scientist can construct the special Academy improvement which, when worked, produces lots of [ICON_RESEARCH] Science. Further, the Great Scientist lets you immediately learn any available technology. Finally, the Great Scientist can start a Golden Age. The Great Scientist is expended when used in any of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_WAR_INFANTRY_STRATEGY: Great War Infantry is the basic Modern era combat unit. It's significantly stronger than its predecessor, the Rifleman.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREAT_WRITER_STRATEGY: A Great Writer can create a Great Work of Writing (generates both [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_TOURISM] Tourism) that is placed in the nearest city that has an appropriate building with an empty slot (like an Amphitheater, National Epic, Heroic Epic, or Royal Library). A Great Writer can also write a Political Treatise, which grants the player a large amount of Culture. Great Writers are expended when used either of these ways.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREEK_COMPANIONCAVALRY_STRA: Companion Cavalry are Greek unique units, replacing the Horseman. Companion Cavalry are faster and more powerful than the Horseman unit, making them the most dangerous cavalry units until the arrival of the Knight. Companion Cavalry help generate Great Generals more rapidly than other units. In addition, they can move after attacking. Companion Cavalry are mounted, and still vulnerable to Spearmen and Pikemen attack.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GREEK_HOPLITE_STRATEGY: The Hoplite is the Greek unique unit, replacing the Spearman. It is far more powerful than the Spearman. The Hoplite is deadly against mounted opponents.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_GUIDED_MISSILE_STRATEGY: The Guided Missile is a one-shot unit which is destroyed when it attacks an enemy target. The Guided Missile may be based in a player-owned friendly city or aboard a nuclear submarine or missile cruiser. They may move from base to base or attack an enemy unit or city with their range of "8". See the rules on Missiles for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELICOPTER_GUNSHIP_STRATEGY: The Helicopter Gunship is a deadly air unit that in most respects acts like any ground unit, except that it cannot capture cities. Its movement of 6 makes it extremely quick, and it can move over all terrain types including mountains as if they were clear, but it must embark to cross water. Gunships receive a hefty combat bonus when fighting tanks.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ANTI_AIRCRAFT_GUN: Will intercept enemy aircraft which try to bomb nearby Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ANTI_TANK_GUN: Relatively cheap Unit specialized in fighting Tanks.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ARCHAEOLOGIST: Archaeologists are a special subtype of Worker that are used to excavate Antiquity Sites to either create Landmark improvements or to extract Artifacts to fill in Great Work of Art slots in Museums, Palaces, Hermitages, and selected Wonders. Archaeologists may work in territory owned by any player. They are consumed once they complete an Archaeological Dig at an Antiquity Site. Archaeologists may not be purchased with [ICON_GOLD] Gold and may only be built in a city with a University.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ARCHER: Powerful Ancient Era ranged Unit that can strike foes from afar.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ARTILLERY: The first Siege Unit in the game capable of firing 3 tiles away. Must set up prior to firing.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ASSYRIAN_SIEGE_TOWER: Powerful melee siege unit which grants a city attack bonus to nearby units when next to an enemy city. May only be built by the Assyrians.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ATOMIC_BOMB: Unleash nuclear destruction upon your foes. Does great damage to Cities and damages any Unit caught in the blast radius. May be stationed on Aircraft Carriers.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Requires Manhattan Project and a Uranium resource.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_AUSTRIAN_HUSSAR: A fast Mounted Unit used for scouting and hit-and-run attacks. Especially deadly with flank attacks. Only the Austrians may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_AXMAN: The Barbarians' replacement of the Chariot Archer.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_B17: Air Unit that rains death from above onto enemy Units and Cities. Only the Americans may build it. This Unit has a chance of evading interception, and does additional damage to Cities compared to the Bomber, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BALLISTA: Siege Unit that does major damage from a distance, but must set up prior to firing. Only the Romans may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_RANGE_STRENGTH] Ranged Combat Strength than the Catapult, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BARBARIAN_WARRIOR: The Barbarians' most basic early-game military Unit.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BATTLESHIP: Most powerful direct-combat naval Unit in the game.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BAZOOKA: Atomic era ranged Unit that must be adjacent to an enemy to strike.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BERBER_CAVALRY: Mounted unit that specializes in Desert warfare and protecting Moroccan lands. Receives combat bonuses when fighting both in Desert tiles and Moroccan territory. May only be built by Morocco.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BOMBER: Air Unit that rains death from above onto enemy Units and Cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BRAZILIAN_PRACINHA: Modern Era infantry unit that earns points toward a [ICON_GOLDEN_AGE] Golden Age when it defeats an enemy. May only be built by Brazil.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BYZANTINE_CATAPHRACT: Fast and powerful early game Mounted Unit. Weak to Spearmen. Only the Byzantines may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength, defends better, and attacks cities more effectively than the Horseman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_BYZANTINE_DROMON: Dominant Ancient Era Naval Unit used to own the seas through the Classic Era with its ranged attack. Only the Byzantines may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CAMEL_ARCHER: Fast Medieval ranged Unit, weak to Pikemen. Only the Arabians may build it. It is a ranged unit, unlike the Knight which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CANNON: Powerful Renaissance Era Siege Unit. Must set up prior to firing.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CARAVAN: Establishes land-based Trade Routes between two civilizations that yields [ICON_GOLD] Gold. Can also be used to deliver [ICON_FOOD] Food or [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production within your own borders after building a Granary or a Workshop.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CARAVEL: Fast Renaissance exploration Unit with a large sight radius.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CARGO_SHIP: Establishes sea-based Trade Routes between two civilizations that yields [ICON_GOLD] Gold. Can also be used to deliver [ICON_FOOD] Food or [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production within your own borders after building a Granary or a Workshop.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CARTHAGINIAN_FOREST_EL: Powerful early game Mounted Unit. Weak to Spearmen. Only the Carthaginians may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength than the Horseman which it replaces and strikes fear into nearby enemy units. Helps produce Great Generals more quickly.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CARTHAGINIAN_QUINQUERE: Strong Ancient Era Naval Unit used to dominate the seas through melee attacks on naval units and cities. Only the Carthaginians may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CATAPULT: First siege Unit of the game. Does major damage to Units and Cities from a distance, but must set up prior to firing.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CAVALRY: Mounted Unit of the Renaissance Era, capable of fighting toe-to-toe with contemporary infantry units. Weak against other Mounted Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CELT_PICTISH_WARRIOR: Only the Celts may build this unit. It has a combat bonus outside of friendly territory and can pillage enemy improvements at no additional movement cost. Earns 50% of opponents' strength as [ICON_PEACE] Faith for kills.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CHARIOR_ARCHER: A fast Ranged Unit highly effective on flat ground, but slowed significantly when entering rough terrain.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_CHUKONU: Medieval ranged infantry Unit. Only the Chinese may build it. This Unit may fire twice in one turn, unlike the Crossbowman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_COMPANION_CAVALRY: Fast and powerful early game Mounted Unit. Weak to Spearmen. Only the Greeks may build it. This Unit is faster, has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength and helps produce Great Generals more quickly than the Horseman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_COSSACK: Mounted Unit of the Renaissance Era, capable of fighting toe-to-toe with contemporary infantry units. Weak against other Mounted Units.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Only the Russians may build it. This Unit has a combat bonus when fighting enemies that have already taken damage.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_DESTROYER: Fast late game ship making it highly effective at recon. Also used to hunt down and destroy enemy Submarines.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_EGYPTIAN_WAR_CHARIOT: A fast Ranged Unit on flat ground, but slowed when entering rough terrain. Only the Egyptians may build it. This Unit does not require [ICON_RES_HORSE] Horses to be built, unlike the Chariot Archer which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_FIGHTER: Air Unit designed to wrest control of the skies and intercept incoming enemy aircraft.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_FOREIGN_LEGION: Basic Infantry Unit of the Industrial Era. Only the French may build it. This Unit has a combat bonus outside of friendly territory, unlike the Infantry which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_FRIGATE: Powerful Renaissance Era naval Unit used to wrest control of the seas.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_GALLEASS: Medieval Era Naval Unit used to wrest control of the seas with its ranged attack.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_GATLINGGUN: Ranged infantry Unit of the mid-game that must be adjacent to an enemy to strike.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_GREAT_WAR_INFANTRY: Basic Infantry Unit of the Modern Era.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_GUIDED_MISSILE: A cheap Unit that may be used once to damage enemy Units or Cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_HELICOPTER_GUNSHIP: Unit specialized in fighting Modern Armor and Tanks. It is capable of hovering over mountains.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_HOPLITE: Ancient Era Unit which specializes in defeating Mounted Units. Only the Greeks may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength than the Spearman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_HORSEMAN: Fast and powerful early game Mounted Unit. Weak to Spearmen.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_HUN_BATTERING_RAM: Siege unit deadly at melee attacks versus cities. Only the Huns may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_HUN_HORSE_ARCHER: A fast Ranged Unit used for hit-and-run attacks. Only the Huns may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_IMMORTAL: Ancient Era Unit which specializes in defeating Mounted Units. Only the Persians may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength and heals more quickly than the Spearman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_INDIAN_WAR_ELEPHANT: A fast Ranged Unit highly effective on flat ground. Only the Indians may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat and Ranged Strength than the Chariot Archer, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_INDONESIAN_KRIS_SWORDS: Classical Era Melee unit that has a mystical weapon whose abilities will be discovered the first time it is used in combat. May only be build by Indonesia.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_INFANTRY: Basic Infantry Unit of the Industrial Era.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_INQUISITOR: Used to remove other religions from cities. May only be obtained by purchasing with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_IRONCLAD: More powerful than Frigates, these Units pay for their strength by being slower and unable to enter ocean tiles.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_JAGUAR: Fairly weak early-game military Unit. Only the Aztecs may build it. This Unit fights more effectively in Jungle and heals damage when it kills an enemy Unit, unlike the Warrior which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_JANISSARY: One of the first gunpowder units of the game. Only the Ottomans may build it. This Unit is stronger on offense and heals when it kills an enemy, unlike the Musketman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_JET_FIGHTER: Air Unit designed to wrest control of the skies and intercept incoming enemy aircraft.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_KNIGHT: Powerful Medieval Mounted Unit, weak to Pikemen.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_LANCER: Mounted Unit designed to be on the attack, and receives a combat penalty when on defense. Good for hunting down enemy Mounted Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_LANDSKNECHT: Specialized in defeating Knights and other mounted Units. Only the Germans may build it. This Unit is much cheaper than the Pikeman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_LONGBOWMAN: Medieval ranged infantry Unit. Only the English may build it. This Unit has 1 additional range compared to the Crossbowman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MACHINE_GUN: Late-game ranged Unit that must be adjacent to an enemy to strike.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MARINE: Atomic Era Unit especially useful for embarking and invading across the sea.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MAYAN_ATLATLIST: Only the Maya may build this unit. It is cheaper and available sooner than the Archer, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MECH: Doesn't benefit from any defensive terrain bonuses, but is otherwise stronger than any other military unit in the game.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MECHANIZED_INFANTRY: Fast, basic Unit of the late-game that requires no special resources to build.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MEHAL_SEFARI: Strong, front-line land Unit that specializes in fighting in rough terrain and defending near the Ethiopian capital. Only the Ethiopians may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MINUTEMAN: One of the first gunpowder units of the game. Only the Americans may build it. This Unit may move through rough ground as though it were flat, unlike the Musketman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MISSILE_CRUISER: Naval Unit specialized in warding off enemy aircraft. Can also hold 3 Missiles.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MISSIONARY: Used to spread a religion to cities. May only be obtained by purchasing with [ICON_PEACE] Faith.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MOBILE_SAM: Will intercept enemy aircraft which try to bomb nearby Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MODERN_ARMOR: Fast and powerful late-game Unit. Receives a penalty when attacking Cities and on defense, but is incredibly effective in open ground.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MOHAWK_WARRIOR: Strong, front-line land Unit of the Classical Era. Only the Iroquois may build it. This Unit receives a major bonus when fighting on Forest and Jungle, unlike the Swordsman which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MUSKETEER: One of the first gunpowder units of the game. Only the French may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength than the Musketman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MUSKETMAN: First gunpowder Unit of the game. Fairly cheap and powerful.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_MUSLIM_CAVALRY: Powerful Medieval Mounted Unit, weak to Pikemen. Only the Songhai may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_NUCLEAR_MISSILE: Unleash nuclear destruction upon your foes. Does great damage to Cities and damages any Unit caught in the blast radius.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Requires Manhattan Project and two Uranium resources.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_NUCLEAR_SUBMARINE: Invisible to all Units except Destroyers and other Submarines, or until it attacks or is adjacent to another unit. Can carry 2 Missiles, and is specialized in hunting other Submarines.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_PANZER: Fast and powerful Industrial Era Unit. Receives a penalty when attacking Cities and on defense, but is incredibly effective in open ground.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Only the Germans may build it. This Unit has 1 additional movement compared to the Tank, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_PARATROOPER: Strong infantry Unit capable of paradropping up to 5 tiles from friendly territory. Paratrooper can also move and pillage after paradropping, but cannot enter combat until the following turn.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_PIKEMAN: Specialized in defeating Knights and other mounted Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_PORTUGUESE_NAU: Melee naval unit which excels at sea exploration. It has more [ICON_MOVES] Movement than the Caravel, which it replaces, and can perform a one-time ability next to foreign lands to earn [ICON_GOLD] Gold and XP. May only be built by the Portuguese.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_PRIVATEER: Naval Unit that specializes in attacking coastal cities to earn gold and capturing enemy ships.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_RIFLEMAN: Strong, front-line land Unit of the mid-game.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ROCKET_ARTILLERY: Fast Siege Unit of the late-game. Unlike other Siege Units, it does not need to set up prior to firing.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ROMAN_LEGION: Strong, front-line land Unit of the Classical Era. Only the Romans may build it. This Unit has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength than the Swordsman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SAMURAI: Powerful Medieval infantry Unit. Only the Japanese may build it. This Unit fights more effectively and helps produce Great Generals more quickly than the Longswordsman, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SCOUT: Excellent at exploring, this Unit is not slowed by rough terrain, but is vulnerable to attack.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SEA_BEGGAR: Naval Unit that specializes in attacking coastal cities to earn gold and capturing enemy ships. Only the Dutch can build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SETTLER: Founds new Cities to expand your empire.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][ICON_FOOD] Growth of the City is stopped while this Unit is being built.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SHIPOFTHELINE: Powerful Renaissance Era Naval Unit used to wrest control of the seas. Only the English may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SHOSHONE_COMANCHE_RIDE: Mounted unit that specializes in quick attacks. May only be built by the Shoshone.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SHOSHONE_PATHFINDER: Excellent at exploring and fearsome in combat, this Shoshone Unique Unit replaces the Scout. It possesses a promotion that allows it to choose the benefit when uncovering an ancient ruin.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SIAMESE_WARELEPHANT: Powerful Medieval Mounted Unit, weak to Pikemen. Only the Siamese may build it. This Unit receives a bonus against other mounted Units and has a higher [ICON_STRENGTH] Combat Strength than the Knight, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SIPAHI: Mounted Unit designed to be on the attack, and receives a combat penalty when on defense. Good for hunting down enemy Mounted Units. May only be built by the Ottomans. This Unit is faster and can see further than the Lancer, which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SPEARMAN: Ancient Era Unit which specializes in defeating Mounted Units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SS_BOOSTER: One of the Spaceship parts that must be built and sent to the capital to win a [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Science Victory[ENDCOLOR]. Unlike other parts, three SS Boosters are needed to complete the spaceship.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SS_PART: One of the Spaceship parts that must be built and sent to the capital to win a [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Science Victory[ENDCOLOR].
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_STEALTH_BOMBER: A long-range Air Unit that rains death from above onto enemy Units and Cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SUBMARINE: Invisible to all Units except Destroyers and other Submarines, or until it attacks or is adjacent to another unit. Can cause major damage to other ships if they get into range without being seen.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SWEDISH_CAROLEAN: Strong, front-line land Unit that specializes in long marches, even far from Swedish lands. Only the Swedish may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SWEDISH_HAKKAPELIITTA: Elite mounted Unit that specializes in working with Great Generals. Only the Swedish may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_SWORDSMAN: Strong, front-line land Unit of the Classical Era.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_TANK: Fast and powerful Industrial Era Unit. Receives a penalty when attacking Cities and on defense, but is incredibly effective in open ground.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_TREBUCHET: Powerful Medieval Siege Unit, good at reducing the defenses of enemy Cities. Must set up prior to firing.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_TRIPLANE: Early Air Unit designed to intercept incoming enemy aircraft.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_TRIREME: Ancient Era Naval Unit used to wrest control of the seas.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_VENETIAN_GALLEASS: A brutally powerful Medieval Era Naval Unit used to wrest control of the seas with its ranged attack. Better at attacking and defending than the normal Galleass it replaces, but more expensive. May only be built by Venice.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_VENETIAN_MERCHANT: This Venetian unique Great Person replaces the Great Merchant. Aside from the ability to Conduct a Trade Mission, the Merchant of Venice can purchase City-States outright, bringing them under Venetian control as a Puppet.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_WINGED_HUSSAR: Heavy Mounted Unit specialized in defeating enemy mounted units. This unit is faster and more powerful than the Lancer, which it replaces. Forces defenders to retreat when it inflicts more damage than it receives. May only be built by the Polish.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_WORKBOAT: Creates Improvements on water resources like [ICON_RES_FISH] Fish. The Work Boat is consumed in the process. It is defenseless, and will be killed if it comes into contact with an enemy.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_WORKER: Improves [ICON_FOOD] Food [ICON_PRODUCTION] Production and [ICON_GOLD] Gold by constructing Improvements on map tiles outside of Cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_WWI_BOMBER: Early Air Unit that can bombard enemy Units and Cities from the skies.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_WWI_TANK: The first of its kind, this powerful late game unit can move after attacking.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_XCOM_SQUAD: Futuristic infantry unit skilled at dropping into danger from far across the world. Squad can move and pillage after landing but cannot enter combat until the following turn.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][COLOR_CYAN]Good Luck, Commander![ENDCOLOR]
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ZERO: Air Unit designed to wrest control of the skies and intercept incoming enemy aircraft. Only the Japanese may build it. This Unit has a bonus against other Fighters, unlike the Fighter which it replaces.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HELP_ZULU_IMPI: Specialized in defeating Knights and other mounted units. Only the Zulu may build it. Also performs a first-strike ranged attack before combat.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HORSEMAN_STRATEGY: The Horseman is the first really powerful cavalry unit, combining speed with a powerful melee attack. The Horseman can move after attacking. As a mounted unit, the Horseman remains vulnerable to attacks from Spearmen, Pikemen and the like.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HUN_BATTERING_RAM_STRATEGY: Battering Rams are a Hunnic unique unit replacing the Spearman. Use Battering Rams to knock down the defenses of a city. They have the Cover I promotion to keep them safe from ranged units; attack them with melee units to defeat them.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_HUN_HORSE_ARCHER_STRATEGY: Horse Archers are fast ranged units, deadly on open terrain. They are slightly stronger in defense than the Chariot Archer and can move through rough terrain without the Chariot Archer's movement penalty. They also start with the Accuracy I promotion. As a mounted unit, the Horse Archer is vulnerable to Spearmen. Unlike the Chariot Archer, the Horse Archer does not require Horses.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_INDIAN_WARELEPHANT_STRATEGY: The War Elephant is the Indian unique unit, replacing the Chariot Archer. It is more powerful (if slower) than the Chariot Archer and can move through any terrain without the Chariot Archer's penalty. A mounted unit, the War Elephant is quite vulnerable to the Spearman.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_INDONESIAN_KRIS_SWORDSMAN_S: The Kris Swordsman replaces the standard Swordsman. Try him out in combat as soon as possible to unlock the secret properties of his curvy Kris blade.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_INFANTRY_STRATEGY: Infantry is the basic Industrial era combat unit. It's significantly stronger than its predecessor, the Rifleman. Modern combat is increasingly complex, and on its own an Infantry unit is vulnerable to air, artillery and tank attack. When possible Infantry should be supported by artillery, tanks, and air (or anti-air) units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_INQUISITOR_STRATEGY: Can be purchased with [ICON_PEACE] Faith in any city with a majority Religion that has been enhanced. They can remove other religions from your cities (expending the Inquisitor) or be placed inside a city to protect it from Missionaries and Prophets trying to spread other religions into that city.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_IRONCLAD_STRATEGY: Ironclads are the first steam-powered warships available. They are harder to kill than any sailing vessel, and their Ranged Combat strength is higher. However Ironclads cannot enter deep ocean hexes. Use them primarily for in-shore defense.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_IROQUOIAN_MOHAWKWARRIOR_STR: The Mohawk Warrior is the Iroquoian unique unit, replacing the Swordsman. As powerful as the Swordsman in Clear or Hilly terrain, the Mohawk Warrior receives a significant combat bonus in Forest and Jungle. Also, unlike the Swordsman, it does not require Iron to build.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_JAPANESE_SAMURAI_STRATEGY: The Samurai are the Japanese unique unit, replacing the Longswordsman. As powerful as the Swordsman, the Samurai automatically receives the "Shock I" promotion, giving it a bonus when fighting in open terrain. Success in combat with a Samurai has an increased chance of generating Great Generals.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_JAPANESE_ZERO_STRATEGY: The Zero is the Japanese unique unit, replacing the fighter. The Zero is a moderately-powerful air unit. It is like the standard fighter, except that it gets a significant combat bonus when battling other fighters. It can be based in any city you own or aboard an aircraft carrier. It can move from city to city (or carrier) and can perform "missions" within its range of 8 tiles. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_JET_FIGHTER_STRATEGY: The Jet Fighter is a powerful air unit. It can be based in any city you own or aboard an aircraft carrier. It can move from base to base and can perform "missions" within its range of "10". Use Jet Fighters to attack enemy aircraft and ground units, to scout enemy positions, and to defend against enemy air attacks. Jet Fighters are especially effective against enemy helicopters. The Jet Fighter has the "air recon" ability, which means that everything within 6 tiles of its starting location is visible at the beginning of the turn. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_KNIGHT_STRATEGY: The Knight is a fast and powerful unit, the undisputed king of the medieval battlefield. As a mounted unit it is vulnerable to Pikemen, but it can crush other opponents with relative ease. The Knight can move after it attacks, allowing it to make holes in the enemy lines and then ride straight through them.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_LANCER_STRATEGY: The Lancer is the Renaissance horse unit that comes between the Knight and the Cavalry unit. It's faster and more powerful than the Knight, able to sweep those once-mighty units from the map. The Lancer can even take on the powerful Cavalry unit once it appears. However, the Lancer is strictly an offensive weapon; it gets a combat penalty when defending.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_LONGSWORDSMAN_STRATEGY: The Longswordsman is the standard medieval infantry combat unit. It is almost as powerful as the Knight, but much slower. It is more powerful than the Pikeman, but doesn't receive that unit's bonus versus mounted units. Use Longswordsmen and Catapults to attack enemy cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MACHINE_GUN_STRATEGY: The Machine Gun is the penultimate non-siege ranged unit. It is more powerful than earlier ranged units. Like the Gatling Gun it has only a one-hex range, but still can deal significant damage to other units without being hurt itself. It is vulnerable to melee attack. Put Machine Guns in your city for defense.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MARINE_STRATEGY: The Marine is an alternative to Infantry. It costs a bit more than Infantry but possesses promotions that enhance its Sight and attack strength when embarked at sea.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MAYAN_ATLATLIST_STRATEGY: The Atlatlist is the Mayan unique unit, replacing the Archer. Atlatlists are both cheaper than a traditional Archer, and available earlier without having to research Archery. This early game advantage allows other research priorities to come into play.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MECH_STRATEGY: The "mech" is a Modern Armor unit on steroids. It has a shockingly high Combat Strength, allowing it to stomp virtually any unit it meets into paste. It can also move after attacking. The GDR doesn't benefit from any defensive terrain bonuses, but frankly, who cares? It's really cool!
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MECHANIZED_INFANTRY_STRATEG: Mechanized Infantry are stronger and faster than regular Infantry. Although fast, Mech Infantry cannot move after combat like Tanks; their primary responsibility is, as always, to take and hold the ground.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MEHAL_SEFARI_STRATEGY: Mehal Sefari are the backbone of the Ethiopian army. They start with promotions that give them bonuses fighting in rough terrain and near their capital. They are slightly cheaper to build than the Rifleman that they replace.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MISSILE_CRUISER_STRATEGY: The Missile Cruiser is a modern warship. It's fast and tough, carries a mean Ranged Combat punch and is pretty good at intercepting enemy aircraft. Most importantly, the Missile Cruiser can carry Guided Missiles and Nuclear Missiles, allowing you to carry these deadly weapons right up to the enemy's shore. Missile Cruisers combined with carriers and submarines make a fiendishly powerful armada.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MISSIONARY_STRATEGY: Can be purchased with [ICON_PEACE] Faith in any city with a majority Religion. Once adjacent to a city they can spread their religion to it (expending the Missionary after the second use). If a Missionary enters another civilization without Open Borders, their Religious Strength is reduced each turn. Once their Religious Strength reaches zero, they are destroyed.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MOBILE_SAM_STRATEGY: Mobile SAM (surface-to-air) units provide an advancing army with anti-air defense. Mobile SAM units can intercept and shoot at enemy aircraft bombing targets within 2 hexes (but only one unit per turn). These units are fairly vulnerable to non-air attack and should be accompanied by infantry or armor.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MODERN_ARMOR_STRATEGY: Modern Armor is just like its predecessor, the Tank, only more-so. Modern Armor is much stronger than the Tank unit, although it still suffers the Tank's penalty when attacking cities. Modern Armor can continue to move after combat.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_MUSKETMAN_STRATEGY: The Musketman is the first gunpowder unit in the game, and it replaces all of the older foot-soldier types - Crossbowmen, Longswordsmen, Pikemen, and the like. It is not a ranged unit, however, and cannot attack an enemy that is more than one hex away.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_NUCLEAR_MISSILE_STRATEGY: The Nuclear Missile is an upgraded, more powerful Atomic Bomb. The Nuclear Missile can be based in any city you own or aboard a Nuclear Submarine or Missile Cruiser. It can move from base to base or attack a target within its range of "12." When it detonates, a Nuclear Missile will damage (and possibly destroy) cities and destroy all units within its blast radius of 2 tiles. It is automatically destroyed when it attacks. See the rules on Nuclear Weapons for more details.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_NUCLEAR_SUBMARINE_STRATEGY: The Nuclear Submarine is tougher and stronger than the Industrial-era model. It too is invisible to most units and can see other submarines. Most importantly, Nuclear Submarines can carry guided missiles and nuclear missiles. Like submarines, Nuclear Subs are vulnerable to enemy destroyers and subs, so send units of your own out ahead to make sure the coast is clear.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_OTTOMAN_JANISSARY_STRATEGY: The Janissary is an Ottoman unique unit, replacing the Musketman. The Janissary receives a significant combat bonus when on the attack. In addition, the Janissary unit automatically heals all damage when it destroys a non-Barbarian unit. This can give a Janissary army a crushing advantage against an enemy force on the defensive.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_OTTOMAN_SIPAHI_STRATEGY: The Sipahi is the Ottoman unique unit, replacing the Lancer. The Sipahi is much faster and can see one hex farther than the Lancer, although it shares the Lancer's weaknesses on defense. The Sipahi can pillage enemy improvements at no additional cost.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_PARATROOPER_STRATEGY: The Paratrooper is a late-game infantryman. It is somewhat weaker than a mechanized infantry unit; however, it can parachute up to 5 tiles away (when starting in friendly territory). This allows the paratrooper to literally jump over enemy positions and destroy road networks, pillage vital resources and so forth, wrecking havoc behind his lines. The Paratrooper is at great risk when on such missions, so make sure the target is worth it!
TXT_KEY_UNIT_PERSIAN_IMMORTAL_STRATEGY: The Immortal is the Persian unique unit, replacing the Spearman. It is slightly more powerful than the Spearman, and it heals twice as quickly. It is quite deadly against mounted units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_PIKEMAN_STRATEGY: The Pikeman is a more powerful Spearman. It receives a bonus when fighting against mounted units, which makes it pretty much the only unit that can hope to stand up against a rampaging Knight. A Pikeman fortified in defensive terrain is a Knight's worst nightmare.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_POLISH_WINGED_HUSSAR_STRATE: The Winged Hussar is the Lancer replacement, and only the Polish may build it. Faster and more powerful than the Lancer, the Winged Hussar forces a defender to retreat when it inflicts more damage than it receives. A defender who cannot retreat instead takes extra damage.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_PORTUGUESE_NAU_STRATEGY: This melee naval unit replaces the Caravel and is skilled at exploration. When it is in a tile next to land owned by another Civilization or City-State you are at peace with, it may perform a one-time trade mission to earn [ICON_GOLD] Gold and XP. The further the land is from your own capital, the greater the bonus, so it is best to use on distant players. Use this unit to explore the map and uncover the locations of other players.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_PRIVATEER_STRATEGY: Naval Unit that attacks as a melee unit. Starts with the first Coastal Raider promotion allowing it to earn gold from attacking coastal cities. May capture any enemy naval units it defeats as prize ships.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_RIFLEMAN_STRATEGY: The Rifleman is the gunpowder unit following the musketman. It is significantly more powerful than the musketman, giving the army with the advanced units a big advantage over civs which have not yet upgraded to the new unit.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ROCKET_ARTILLERY_STRATEGY: Rocket Artillery is an extremely powerful siege weapon, far superior to the standard artillery. Rocket Artillery does not need to set up before firing, making it much quicker than earlier guns. Although the Rocket Artillery's sight range is limited, it can use indirect fire to attack targets it cannot see (as long as other friendly units can see them). Like all artillery it is vulnerable to infantry or armor attack, and should be defended by appropriate units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ROMAN_BALLISTA_STRATEGY: Roman unique unit, more powerful than the Catapult it replaces. The Ballista is an excellent siege weapon. It is extremely useful against cities, but it is quite vulnerable to attack. Be sure to protect the Ballista with other military units. The Ballista must first "set up" (1 MP) before attacking.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ROMAN_LEGION_STRATEGY: The Legion is the Roman unique unit, replacing the Swordsman. The Legion is more powerful than the Swordsman, making it the most powerful melee unit of the Classical Era. The Legion can also construct roads and forts and is the only non-Worker unit able to do so.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_RUSSIAN_COSSACK_STRATEGY: The Cossack is a Russian unique unit, replacing the Cavalry. They are the same strength as Cavalry and can also move after attacking. However, Cossack units get a strong combat bonus when battling damaged units. This makes Cossacks exceptionally strong at counter-attacking - let the enemy chew up the front-line peasants, then tear the weakened foe apart with a Cossack charge!
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SCOUT_STRATEGY: This early unit can attack but is weak. However, it can move through difficult terrain as though it were clear (all tiles cost 1mp per hex). Use it for world exploration.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SEA_BEGGAR_STRATEGY: Naval Unit used to specializes in attacking coastal cities to earn gold and capturing enemy ships. Only the Dutch may build it. Heals outside friendly territory. Starts with the first two Coastal Raider promotions allowing it to earn gold from attacking coastal cities. May capture any enemy naval units it defeats as prize ships.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SETTLER_STRATEGY: Use this unit to construct new cities. It cannot defend itself, so accompany it with a combat unit.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SHOSHONE_COMANCHE_RIDERS_ST: The Comanche Riders replaces the Cavalry, and only the Shoshone may build it. Cheaper and faster than the base unit.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SHOSHONE_PATHFINDER_STRATEG: The Pathfinder is the Scout replacement, and only the Shoshone may build it. It explores as well as a Scout and fights almost as well as a Warrior. It allows the player to choose the benefit when uncovering an ancient ruin. Costs more than a Scout.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SIAMESE_WARELEPHANT_STRATEG: This is the Siamese unique unit, replacing the Knight. The Naresuan's Elephant unit is slower than a Knight but it is more powerful. Like the Knight the Naresuan's Elephant can move after attacking. Equipped with spears, the Elephant gets a significant combat bonus when attacking other mounted units.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SONGHAI_MUSLIMCAVALRY_STRAT: This is a Songhai unique unit, replacing the Knight. This unit has the same statistics as the Knight, but it gains a significant bonus when attacking cities. The Mandekalu Cavalry can move after attacking. Its speed makes it difficult for an enemy to build a defensive line before the Mandekalu Cavalry reaches the target.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SPEARMAN_STRATEGY: The Spearman is the first melee unit available after the Warrior. It is just slightly more powerful than the Warrior. However, it gets a significant combat bonus against mounted units (Chariot Archer, Horsemen and so forth).
TXT_KEY_UNIT_STEALTH_BOMBER_STRATEGY: The Stealth Bomber is an upgraded bomber, possessing increased range, a greater Ranged Combat Strength, and an increased ability to avoid enemy anti-aircraft and fighters. The Stealth Bomber has the "air recon" ability, which means that everything within 6 tiles of its starting location is visible at the beginning of the turn. Note that Stealth Bombers cannot be transported aboard Carriers. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SUBMARINE_STRATEGY: The Submarine is a specialized ship-killing vessel. It is invisible to most units - except for destroyers and other Submarines - until it attacks, or is adjacent to another unit. The Submarine can enter ice hexes. Use the Submarine to patrol enemy waters and sink enemy ships, and to guard your own ships against attacks from enemy subs.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SWEDISH_CAROLEAN_STRATEGY: Caroleans are the backbone of the early Industrial era Swedish army. They start with the March promotion that allows it to Heal every turn, even if it performs an action.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SWEDISH_HAKKAPELIITTA_STRAT: Hakkapeliitta are the elite mounted units of the late Renaissance Swedish army. Stack a Great General with them if possible. The Great General receives the movement allowance of the Hakkapeliitta if they start the turn stacked. In addition, the Hakkapeliitta receive a 15% combat bonus when stacked with a Great General.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_SWORDSMAN_STRATEGY: The Swordsman is a powerful standard melee unit during the Classical Era, much stronger than the Spearman and Warrior. They are very useful when attacking early-era cities.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_TANK_STRATEGY: The Tank is an extremely powerful and mobile land unit. It can move after combat, allowing it to blow holes in enemy lines and then move into the now-vacant hexes. The Tank receives a small penalty when attacking enemy cities, so it's often a good idea to spearhead the attack with the Tank and then follow up with artillery and infantry.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_TREBUCHET_STRATEGY: A Trebuchet is a medieval siege weapon, significantly more powerful than the earlier Catapult units. The Trebuchet must expend 1mp to set up, but once it is in position it can do serious damage to a fortified enemy city. Defend your trebuchets with melee and ranged units to keep enemy units from getting too close.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_TRIPLANE_STRATEGY: The Triplane is an early air unit. It can be based in any city you own or aboard an aircraft carrier. It can move from city to city (or carrier) and can perform "missions" within its range of 5 tiles. Use triplanes to attack enemy aircraft and ground units, to scout enemy positions, and to defend against enemy air attacks. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_TRIREME_STRATEGY: The Trireme is the first naval unit available to civilizations in the game. It is a ranged unit with a modest Ranged Combat Strength. It cannot initiate melee combat. The Trireme is useful for clearing barbarian ships out of home waters and for limited exploration (it cannot enter Deep Ocean hexes outside of the city borders unless it is from Polynesia).
TXT_KEY_UNIT_VENETIAN_GALLEASS_STRATEGY: The Great Galleass is the Galleass replacement, and only the Venetians may build it. Has a stronger ranged attack, is more resilient in battle, and is more costly than the base Galleass.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_VENETIAN_MERCHANT_STRATEGY: The Merchant of Venice is a unique Great Merchant replacement. Aside from the normal Great Merchant abilities, the Merchant of Venice can acquire a City-State outright, bringing it under Venetian control as a puppet. The Merchant of Venice is expended when used in this way.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_WARRIOR_STRATEGY: The Warrior is the earliest combat unit available, useful for battling barbarians, early city defense and limited exploration. Warriors are generally not strong enough to take enemy cities, unless heavily supported by Archers and Catapults - and even then they may simply not have the strength for the task.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_WORK_BOAT_STRATEGY: This unit can only be constructed in coastal cities. Use work boats to improve water resources - fish, whales, oil, etc. The work boat is consumed when it constructs the improvement. Work boats can't enter ocean hexes until Astronomy is discovered. Work boats cannot engage in combat.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_WORKER_STRATEGY: This unit constructs roads, railroads and builds improvements like farms and mines. It cannot defend itself, so accompany it with a military unit when it is in dangerous territory.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_WWI_BOMBER_STRATEGY: The Great War Bomber is an early air unit. It is effective against ground targets, less so against naval targets, and it's quite vulnerable to enemy aircraft. The Great War Bomber can be based on a player-owned city or aboard a carrier. It can move from base to base and perform missions within its range of "6." Use it to attack enemy units and cities. When possible, send in triplanes or fighters first to "use up" the enemy's anti-aircraft defenses for that turn. See the rules on Aircraft for more information.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_WWI_TANK_STRATEGY: The Landship is a powerful and mobile land unit. It can move after combat, allowing it to blow holes in enemy lines and then move into the now-vacant hexes.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_XCOM_SQUAD_STRATEGY: The XCOM Squad is an elite team of soldiers based on the technology pioneered by the XCOM Initiative. When starting the turn in friendly territory this squad can be dropped up to 40 tiles away. Deploy these units into areas where the danger level is out of this world.
TXT_KEY_UNIT_ZULU_IMPI_STRATEGY: Before engaging in melee, the Impi performs a spear throw attack that can damage or kill the enemy unit before making contact. Only the Zulu may build it.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_AIR_HEADING3_BODY: Air units are units which, unsurprisingly, travel through the air. They are critically important during the late game, as control of the skies often determines victory or defeat in modern warfare. They include helicopters, airplanes, and missiles.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_CHARACTERISTICS_ADV_QUEST: What are the characteristics of a unit?
TXT_KEY_UNITS_CHARACTERISTICS_HEADING2_B: All units have three basic statistics ("stats"): movement speed, combat strength, and promotions.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_COMBATSTRENGTH_ADV_QUEST: What is combat strength and how does it work?
TXT_KEY_UNITS_COMBATSTRENGTH_HEADING3_BO: A unit's Combat Strength (CS) determines how powerful it is in combat. The warrior, the earliest combat unit available, has a CS of 6. Non-combat units like settlers and workers have CS's of 0 (zero). They are defeated (captured or destroyed) when attacked by any military unit.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_COMBATUNITS_ADV_QUEST: What are the combat unit types?
TXT_KEY_UNITS_COMBATUNITS_HEADING2_BODY: Combat units are divided into several categories. These include "Melee Units," "Ranged Units," "Naval Units, and "Air Units."
TXT_KEY_UNITS_CONSTRUCTING_HEADING2_BODY: Units are built in cities. Each unit has a certain "Production Cost" which determines how many units of Production a city must expend to produce the unit. In addition, in order to construct a unit your civilization must have knowledge of the requisite technology (you must know the "Archery" technology for example to construct Archer units). Some units also require that your civ have access to certain resources to construct them (Swordsmen require Iron, for instance).
TXT_KEY_UNITS_HEADING1_BODY: In Civilization V, the term "unit" refers to anything that can move around the map. There are a number of different types of units in play - military units, workers, settlers, great people, and the like, with military units forming the bulk of them.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_MOVEMENT_HEADING2_BODY: Generally, units move from hex to hex, paying the "Movement Cost" required to enter the new hex. Units are subject to "Stacking" limitations - two military units may not end their turn in the same hex, nor can two non-military units, but one military and one non-military unit may end their turn stacked in the same hex. Most units are limited in where they can move - land units cannot enter mountain hexes and naval units cannot enter land hexes (except for port cities). Improvements like roads and railroads speed a unit's movement through land hexes.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Check out the section on Movement for details.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_MOVESPEED_HEADING3_BODY: A unit's Movement Points (MPs) determines how many clear tiles a unit can move through. Most early units have 2 MPs. See the Civilopedia section on Movement for more details.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_NATIONAL_HEADING2_BODY: Each civilization in Civilization V has one or more special "national units." These units are unique to that civilization, and they are in some way superior to the standard version of that unit. The American civilization, for example, has a Minuteman unit, which is superior to the standard Musketman available to other civs, and it also possesses the B-17, replacing the Bomber unit. The Greek civ has the Hoplite and Companion Cavalry units, which replace the Spearman and Horseman other civs get.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]See each civilization's Civilopedia entry to discover its special unit.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_NAVAL_HEADING3_BODY: Naval units are units that can move in water hexes. They cannot enter land hexes, except for coastal cities. Depending upon its type, a naval unit may be limited to travel in coastal waters, or it may be able to enter deep water Ocean hexes. Naval units are Ranged Combat Units.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_NONCOMBAT_HEADING2_BODY: There are five types of non-combat units: Settlers, Workers, Work Boats, Great People, and Spaceship Parts. Each is critically important to a civilization's success. As the name "non-combat" would suggest, these units cannot fight. If attacked by an enemy unit while alone in a hex they are automatically captured or destroyed. Therefore it usually makes sense to escort them with a military unit when sending them out into the wilderness.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_SPECIALABILITIES_ADV_QUEST: What are a unit's special abilities?
TXT_KEY_UNITS_SPECIALABILITIES_HEADING2_: Many units have special abilities, allowing them to do things better than other units, or to do things that other units cannot do at all. Settler units can found new cities, for example, and no other units can do these things. An archer unit can deal "ranged" damage, allowing it to attack an enemy that is not adjacent to it, while most combat units cannot. Check out a unit's Civilopedia entry to see its special abilities.
TXT_KEY_UNITS_UNITCOMBAT_HEADING2_BODY: Military units can engage in combat against other units or against cities. Most military units are "melee units," meaning that they can attack only enemies in hexes directly adjacent to them. Some military units are "ranged units," meaning that they can attack enemies one or more hexes away.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]See the section on Combat for details.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_2050ARRIVES_ADV_QUEST: What happens when the date hits 2050?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_2050ARRIVES_HEADING3_BOD: If the year 2050 arrives and nobody has won one of the victories below, the game ends automatically and the civilization with the most Victory Points wins.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_ADV_QUEST: How can you win and lose the game?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_ANOTHERCIVWINS_ADV_QUEST: What happens if another civilization wins the game?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_ANOTHERCIVWINS_HEADING3_: If another civilization achieves one of the five victories explained below, game over: you lose. It doesn't matter if you were about to achieve your own victory, whoever wins first wins, and everybody else loses.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_CAPITAL_ADV_QUEST: What happens if a capital is lost? Is another one founded?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_CAPITAL_HEADING4_BODY: If your original capital has been captured, another of your cities will automatically be assigned as a replacement capital. This city functions in all ways like the original, except that it can be destroyed, and it does not count towards a Domination victory. If you ever retake your original capital, it will resume its leadership position in your civilization.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_CITYSTATES_HEADING2_BODY: City-States cannot win a game of Civilization V. Only major civilizations can do so.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_CULTURAL_HEADING3_BODY: To win a cultural victory, you must acquire five complete "branches" of social policies - that is, you must own all policies within five different branches. Once you've done that, the "Utopia Project" is unlocked. Construct that project and you win a cultural victory. You cannot rush or purchase this project. It must be constructed.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_DESTROYING_ADV_QUEST: How can I raze an original capital city?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_DESTROYING_HEADING4_BODY: Can't be done. A capital cannot be destroyed by any means. It can be captured, but not destroyed. Dropping a nuke on the city will at worst reduce its population to 1, but the city will not be destroyed. Accept it and move on. (Remember that you can drive another civ out of the game by destroying or capturing all of its cities, so you can still wipe your foes off the map even if you can't erase their capital from the face of the earth...)
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_DIPLOMATIC_HEADING3_BODY: When a player learns the "Globalization" technology, he or she can construct the United Nations. Once that is constructed, a vote will be taken every few turns for the position of World Leader. If a leader gets enough votes to win the position, he or she immediately wins a diplomatic victory. The amount of votes needed to win a diplomatic victory depends upon the number of civilizations in play at the start of the game. The fewer players in the game, the greater the percentage of votes needed to win the game.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_DOMINATION_HEADING3_BODY: If you are the last player in possession of your own original capital you win. So if you capture all other civs' capitals and hang onto your own, you've achieved victory. However, this can be tricky. Suppose you're in a five-player game and you capture three of your opponents' original capitals, but the fifth player sneaks in and captures your capital while you're not paying attention - then he would win immediately. In other words, it doesn't matter who captures what: it's the last player holding onto his original capital who gets the victory.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]If you've lost your original capital, but still possess other cities, you can still win another type of victory: culture, scientific, diplomatic, or time. However, you cannot win a domination victory until and unless you recapture your own original capital.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_ENDOFTIME_HEADING2_BODY: If no one has achieved victory, the game ends automatically at the end of 2050. The score of all surviving civilizations will be tallied and a victor announced. You may continue playing the game after this point, but victory will no longer be a factor.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_HEADING1_BODY: There are multiple paths to victory in Civilization V. You can win through scientific dominance, becoming the first civ to create and launch a spaceship to Alpha Centauri. You can overwhelm the other civilizations through cultural superiority or political cunning. Or you can employ the ever-popular "crush all of your enemies beneath the wheels of your chariot" tactic and win a mighty domination victory. Whichever civilization achieves one set of victory conditions first wins.[NEWLINE]It's important to keep an eye on your opponents' progress toward victory as you advance your own civilization. There's nothing so annoying as to be on the verge of capturing your last surviving enemy's final city, only to watch helplessly as he or she completes her spaceship and wins an upset scientific victory.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_HOWTOLOSE_ADV_QUEST: How can I lose the game?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_HOWTOLOSE_HEADING2_BODY: There are three paths to failure in Civilization V: losing your capital, another civilization winning the game, or not having the highest score at 2050 AD.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_HOWTOWIN_ADV_QUEST: How can you win the game?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_HOWTOWIN_HEADING2_BODY: There are five paths to victory available to you in Civilization V:[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Domination Victory[NEWLINE]Science Victory[NEWLINE]Cultural Victory[NEWLINE]Diplomatic Victory[NEWLINE]2050 Arrives
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_LIBERATION_ADV_QUEST: How does liberation of city-states effect the game?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_LIBERATION_HEADING4_BODY: If you capture a city that belonged to a conquered civilization or city-state, you have the chance to liberate that civilization or city-state, bringing them back into the game. This earns a large amount of admiration from the liberated player which can be leveraged for an attempt to win Diplomatic Victory. For a liberated city-state, you will gain a large amount of influence which goes towards securing them as your ally and gaining more delegates in the World Congress. For a liberated civilization, they will like you much more, and be much more likely to use their delegates to support you for World Leader.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_LOSINGCITY_ADV_QUEST: What happens if you lose your last city?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_LOSINGCITY_HEADING3_BODY: If you lose your last city - to another civilization or to an angry city-state - then you lose immediately. This is very embarrassing, so don't let it happen to you.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_SCIENCE_ADV_QUEST: How does the science victory work?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_SCIENCE_HEADING3_BODY: You achieve a science victory by learning the necessary technologies to create all of the pieces of the spaceship, then building the parts and moving them to your current capital (or building them there in the first place). Once all the parts are together you may launch your ship to Alpha Centauri. If it successfully makes it to a new planet before anyone else achieves a victory condition, you win the Science Victory!
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_SPACESHIP_ADV_QUEST: How do you put together a spaceship for the science victory?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_SPACESHIP_HEADING4_BODY: Spaceship parts are constructed and move around the map like any other vehicle. Each requires an advanced technology to construct. They are non-combat units and are automatically destroyed if captured. Once a part has been constructed, order it to move to your current capital. When it arrives you'll be asked if you want to add it to your spaceship.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]When all spaceship parts have been added, the ship will launch into space and you will have won a science victory![NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Incidentally, spaceship parts cannot be rushed or purchased. They must be constructed in a city.
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_VICTORYPOINTS_ADV_QUEST: What are these victory points that don't exist?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_VICTORYPOINTS_HEADING3_B: You earn points for:[NEWLINE]- The number of tiles in your borders (this is the least important factor in victory)[NEWLINE]- The number of cities in your empire[NEWLINE]- Your population[NEWLINE]- The number of techs you possess[NEWLINE]- The number of "future techs" you possess[NEWLINE]- The number of Wonders you have constructed (this is the most important factor in determining victory)
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_WHOVOTES_ADV_QUEST: Who votes in a diplomatic victory?
TXT_KEY_VICTORY_WHOVOTES_HEADING4_BODY_A: Once the World Congress is founded, civilizations express their diplomatic power through the use of delegates. During each session of the World Congress, you assign these delegates to different resolutions that have different gameplay effects. Once the United Nations is founded, delegates are essential to becoming the World Leader and winning Diplomatic Victory.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]See the "World Congress" topic for more detailed information.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ALHAMBRA_DESC: Found in southern Spain, the Alhambra is a large palace complex of the Nasrid Dynasty, who controlled the region in the mid 14th century. Construction first began sometime around 1238 AD under the rule of Ibn al-Ahmar, and continued under his successors. Taking its name from the red hue of its walls, the name Alhambra is translated as the "Red Fortress." Noted for its Moorish Islamic architectural style, the Alhambra features elaborate engravings and numerous examples of the interlacing patterns known as "Arabesques" found predominately in Islamic art. Although many examples of the original Islamic style remain today, much of the palace and its interior was reconstructed and modified after the rise of Catholicism in Spain.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ALHAMBRA_HELP: All newly-trained non-air Units in this City receive the [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]Drill I[ENDCOLOR] Promotion, improving [ICON_STRENGTH] combat strength when fighting in [COLOR_POSITIVE_TEXT]ROUGH[ENDCOLOR] Terrain. Provides a free Castle in the city in which it is built. Boosts city [ICON_CULTURE] Culture output by 20%.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ALHAMBRA_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"Justice is an unassailable fortress, built on the brow of a mountain which cannot be overthrown by the violence of torrents, nor demolished by the force of armies."[NEWLINE]- Joseph Addison[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ANGKOR_WAT_HELP: [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_GOLD] Gold costs of acquiring new tiles reduced by 25% in every city.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ANGKORWAT_DESC: Angkor Wat is a temple in Angkor, Cambodia. Angkor Wat was constructed in the 12th century by King Suryavaman II to serve as the royal palace and state temple, Hinduism being the state religion. Two or three centuries later Angkor Wat was converted to Buddhist use. Angkor Wat is a complex of buildings surrounded by a wall and then moat. The enclosed area measures 3000 feet by 2600 feet.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Angkor Wat is constructed primarily of sandstone blocks. The complex is laid out in a rectangular pattern somewhat resembling that of an ancient fortress; the inner buildings are separated from the outer walls by a courtyard similar to a bailey one might find in a castle.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]At one time the courtyard was filled with wooden buildings, but these have all since perished. The surviving stone inner structure is split into three galleries built on raised platforms. The outer galleys once contained libraries, while the inner was a shrine to Vishnu (and has since been converted to a shrine to Buddha). The galleries are topped with towers that resemble stylized beehives, the tallest reaching about 140 feet in height. The buildings' walls are covered with ornate and beautiful bas relief sculptures depicting scenes from Hindu mythology.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Though largely neglected in the latter half of the 20th century, serious efforts are now being made to preserve and restore this wonderful and unique place of worship, and pilgrims and tourists are visiting it in ever-greater numbers each year.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_ANGKORWAT_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"The temple is like no other building in the world. It has towers and decoration and all the refinements which the human genius can conceive of."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Antonio da Magdalena[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BIG_BEN_HELP: Cost of [ICON_GOLD] Gold purchasing in all cities reduced by 15%.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BIGBEN_DESC: Big Ben is the clock (and the clock tower) at the northern end of the Houses of Parliament in London, England. Big Ben (the clock) is famous for its accuracy and for its massive bell which weighs more than 13 tons and tolls out the day's hours.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Big Ben was designed by Lord Edmund Grimthorpe and constructed by Edward Dent and his son, Frederick. The clock and bell were installed in the tower in 1859. Big Ben is the second largest four-faced clock in the world. At the base of each face of the clock is the Latin inscription, "Oh Lord, keep safe our Queen Victoria the First."
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BIGBEN_QUOTE: [NEWLINE][TAB][TAB]"To achieve great things, two things are needed: a plan, and not quite enough time."[NEWLINE][TAB][TAB] - Leonard Bernstein[NEWLINE][TAB]
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BOROBUDUR_HELP: Must be built in a Holy City. 3 free Missionaries appear.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BRANDENBURG_GATE_HELP: A Great General appears near the City where the Wonder was built and +15 XP for all Units built in this City.
TXT_KEY_WONDER_BRANDENBURGGATE_DESC: The Brandenburg Gate is the only surviving town gate of the city of Berlin. A beautiful "triumphal arch" constructed in the Greek style in 1788-91, it features many columns and is surmounted by the "Quadriga of Victory," a statue of a chariot drawn by four horses. It stands at the end of the avenue Unter den Linden.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]After World War II, Berlin was partitioned between sections controlled by the Soviet Union and its allies and the United States and its allies, and the Berlin Wall was constructed by the Soviets to keep the two parts of the city separated.
